items.situation: two friends discussing a movie one just saw — good twists but too long

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, I just got back from seeing 'The Chrono Paradox'. Wow, those plot twists really caught me off guard.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh yeah? I've been thinking about catching it this weekend. Was it worth the ticket price?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Absolutely. The storyline is incredibly clever. Honestly, I didn't see the ending coming at all.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That sounds great. Any downsides I should know about?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Well, it's almost three hours long. I'll admit, my legs were getting a bit numb by the final act.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm, that's a long sit. I'll probably grab a seat in the back row so I can stretch out a bit.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Good call. And maybe skip the large soda unless you want to miss a key scene.

questions.stem: What is the conversation mainly about?

options.A: The woman is recommending a movie she enjoyed despite a minor flaw.
options.B: The woman is complaining that the movie was too confusing to follow.
options.C: The man is trying to convince the woman to see a different film.
options.D: The man is asking for directions to the nearest movie theater.

questions.explanation: 正确答案A: 对话主旨是女生推荐她刚看过的电影，虽然有点长，但非常精彩。B选项错误，因为女生没有抱怨电影难懂，而是称赞剧情巧妙。C选项错误，男生没有试图说服女生看别的电影，而是想去看这部。D选项错误，对话没有涉及去电影院的路线。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do when he goes to see the movie?

options.A: Arrive early to avoid the long lines.
options.B: Ask the woman to come with him.
options.C: Choose a seat in the back row.
options.D: Leave before the movie ends to avoid the crowd.

questions.explanation: 正确答案C: 男生明确说“I'll probably grab a seat in the back row so I can stretch out a bit”，所以他会选择后排座位。A选项错误，对话没有提到排队。B选项错误，女生已经看过了，不会再一起去。D选项错误，对话没有说他会在电影结束前离开。

items.situation: a student returning a too-small sweater at a store counter

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'd like to return this sweater I bought yesterday. I tried it on at home and it's way too small.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure, do you have the receipt with you? I can process a refund or an exchange.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I think I do. Let me check my bag... Oh, here it is.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Great. Unfortunately, this sweater was part of a clearance sale. According to our policy, clearance items can only be exchanged, not refunded.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, I didn't realize that. An exchange would be fine. Do you have this in a medium?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me check the back. I think we might have one left. Give me just a minute.

questions.stem: Why does the woman go to the store counter?

options.A: To complain about the quality of the sweater.
options.B: To return a sweater that does not fit her.
options.C: To ask about the store's return policy.
options.D: To buy a different color of the same sweater.

questions.explanation: 正确答案B: 女生明确说“I'd like to return this sweater... it's way too small”，所以她是来退货的，因为尺寸不合适。A选项错误，她没有抱怨质量，只是说太小了。C选项错误，她不是来询问退货政策的，而是来退货的，政策是店员后来解释的。D选项错误，她不是来买不同颜色的，而是来退换同款不同尺码。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Leave the store without the sweater.
options.B: Ask for a refund in cash.
options.C: Complain to the store manager.
options.D: Wait for the man to check for a medium size.

questions.explanation: 正确答案D: 店员说“Let me check the back... Give me just a minute”，所以女生很可能会在店里等店员去查是否有中码。A选项错误，女生已经同意换货，不会空手离开。B选项错误，店员已经说明清仓商品只能换不能退款，所以退款不可能。C选项错误，女生没有表示不满，而是接受了换货方案。

items.situation: two students figuring out a time to meet for a group project

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, we need to get together to work on the history presentation. Does Thursday afternoon work for you?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Thursday? I've got a chemistry lab from two until five. How about Friday morning instead?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Friday morning works for me. I'm free after ten. Where should we meet?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: How about the library? The group study rooms are usually open then.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Sounds good. I'll book a room for ten thirty. Does that give you enough time?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll bring my laptop with the slides I've started.

questions.stem: When will the students meet to work on their project?

options.A: Thursday at two o'clock.
options.B: Friday at ten o'clock.
options.C: Friday at ten thirty.
options.D: Thursday at five o'clock.

questions.explanation: 正确答案C: 女生说“I'll book a room for ten thirty”，男生同意，所以他们会面时间是周五十点半。A选项错误，周四下午两点男生有化学实验，不能见面。B选项错误，女生说她十点后有空，但最终确定的时间是十点半。D选项错误，周四五点男生刚下课，不是约定时间。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely bring to the meeting?

options.A: His laptop with presentation slides.
options.B: A printed copy of the history textbook.
options.C: Snacks for both of them.
options.D: A list of questions for the professor.

questions.explanation: 正确答案A: 男生明确说“I'll bring my laptop with the slides I've started”，所以他最可能带笔记本电脑和已经开始的幻灯片。B选项错误，对话没有提到带课本。C选项错误，没有提到带零食。D选项错误，没有提到要给教授准备问题清单。

items.situation: two friends figuring out a bus or train schedule for a day trip

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So for our trip to the lake this Saturday, should we take the early bus or the train? I know the bus is cheaper.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The train is faster though. It only takes an hour and a half, while the bus is over two hours.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: True. But the bus station is right next to my apartment, so I wouldn't have to walk far.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Well, the train station is closer to me. What if we take the bus, but we leave earlier? There's a 7:15 AM bus.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: 7:15? That's pretty early for a Saturday. But I guess it means we'll have more time at the lake.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. We can grab coffee on the way and be there by nine thirty.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Alright, let's do it. I'll check the bus schedule online and send you the details.

questions.stem: What is the main topic of the conversation?

options.A: Deciding which bus stop is closest to the woman's apartment.
options.B: Complaining about the high cost of train tickets.
options.C: Planning what activities to do at the lake.
options.D: Choosing the best transportation for a weekend trip.

questions.explanation: 正确答案D: 对话的核心是两人讨论周六去湖边玩应该坐公交还是火车，最终决定坐早班公交。A选项错误，虽然提到了公交站离女生公寓近，但这只是讨论中的一个小细节，不是主旨。B选项错误，女生只说公交更便宜，没有抱怨火车票贵。C选项错误，对话没有讨论在湖边做什么活动。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Buy train tickets for Saturday morning.
options.B: Look up the bus departure times online.
options.C: Walk to the train station to check the schedule.
options.D: Call the man to change their plans.

questions.explanation: 正确答案B: 女生最后说“I'll check the bus schedule online and send you the details”，所以她下一步最可能是上网查公交时刻表。A选项错误，他们决定坐公交，不是火车。C选项错误，她会在网上查，不是去车站。D选项错误，她没有要改变计划的意思。

items.situation: two friends discussing whether to join a campus club or intramural team

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'm thinking of joining something this semester. Maybe the photography club or the intramural soccer team.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Both sound fun. What are you leaning towards?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'm not sure. I love taking pictures, but I also miss playing soccer from high school.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Well, the soccer team practices twice a week. The photography club only meets once a month.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, I do have a pretty busy schedule this term. Maybe the club would be easier to manage.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's a good point. And you can still go to the soccer games to watch and support our school.

questions.stem: How often does the photography club meet?

options.A: Once a month.
options.B: Twice a week.
options.C: Once a week.
options.D: Twice a month.

questions.explanation: 正确答案A: 男生明确说“The photography club only meets once a month”，所以摄影俱乐部每月聚会一次。B选项错误，这是足球队的训练频率。C和D选项错误，对话中没有提到这些频率。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely decide to do?

options.A: Join the soccer team and practice twice a week.
options.B: Join both the soccer team and the photography club.
options.C: Join the photography club to save time.
options.D: Decide not to join any club this semester.

questions.explanation: 正确答案C: 女生说“Maybe the club would be easier to manage”，暗示她倾向于选择时间投入更少的摄影俱乐部。A选项错误，她担心日程太忙，所以不太可能选训练更多的足球队。B选项错误，两个都参加会占用更多时间，与她担心日程忙矛盾。D选项错误，她没有表示不参加任何社团。

items.situation: two students swapping music recommendations because one is bored of the same pop singers

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, can you suggest some new music? I'm tired of the same pop singers everyone plays.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, definitely! Try indie folk. There's a band called Wildwood Echo that's been on repeat for me.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Wildwood Echo? Never heard of them. What's their sound like?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Acoustic, soft harmonies, mellow vibes. Their new album just dropped last Friday.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That sounds great. Where can I stream it?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Any major app. I'll text you the playlist tonight.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yes please! I'll listen on my walk tomorrow.

questions.stem: What does the man recommend the woman listen to?

options.A: A new indie folk album from Wildwood Echo
options.B: A jazz playlist his friend just made
options.C: Classical music for studying late at night
options.D: Live recordings from a campus concert series

questions.explanation: 男生明确推荐Wildwood Echo的indie folk新专辑。B是凭空捏造的细节。C完全没在对话中出现。D借用了对话词汇但不相关。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do tonight?

options.A: Download the Wildwood Echo album for the woman
options.B: Walk the woman to her morning class
options.C: Send the woman a playlist by text message
options.D: Buy concert tickets for the new album release

questions.explanation: 男生说今晚会发短信给女生分享歌单，可推知他会发短信。A过度推论。B是话题相关但与对话无关。D是无关延伸。

items.situation: two friends discussing a movie one just saw — good twists but too long

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, did you finally watch 'The Quiet Hour' last weekend? You said you were planning to.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I did! The twists honestly blew me away. There's one near the end I never saw coming.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Really? My roommate said the same thing. So you'd recommend it?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: With one big warning. It runs almost three hours. I started fidgeting around the two-hour mark.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yikes, three hours? I was thinking of seeing it tomorrow night.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Just make sure you've got snacks and a comfortable seat. And maybe skip the giant soda.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: Which movie to watch this coming weekend
options.B: A movie one of them recently saw and enjoyed
options.C: A late-night snack recommendation for the cinema
options.D: How to find comfortable seating at the local theater

questions.explanation: 对话的核心是男生上周末看的电影和给女生的建议。A颠倒了情景。C只是男生最后一句的细节。D借用了对话中的'舒适座位'做表面陷阱。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the woman's plans?

options.A: She has decided to skip the movie entirely
options.B: She will read the book the movie is based on
options.C: She will avoid going to any long movies in the future
options.D: She intends to watch the same movie tomorrow night

questions.explanation: 女生说她考虑明晚去看，并听了男生关于零食和座位的建议，因此可推知她仍打算去看。A与对话相反。B完全没提到。C过度概括。

items.situation: two students planning what cheese and food to buy for a dinner party

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, what cheese should we grab for Saturday's dinner? Marco said he'd bring wine, but we need everything else.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Definitely a soft brie. Maybe a sharp cheddar too — people always reach for both.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: And crackers?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Crackers, fresh grapes, and some olives if the deli has the kalamatas. Skip nuts — Sara has the allergy.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right, good call. Should we get one block or two?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Two blocks for sure. Eight guests will tear through one in twenty minutes.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Got it. I'll handle the cheese run after class.

questions.stem: What does the man suggest they avoid buying?

options.A: The kalamata olives at the deli counter
options.B: Both brie and sharp cheddar varieties
options.C: Any nuts because of a guest's allergy
options.D: Crackers because they often go stale fast

questions.explanation: 男生明确说skip nuts因为Sara过敏。A是男生推荐买的。B也是男生推荐的。D凭空捏造的理由。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do after class?

options.A: Stop at the store to pick up the cheese and snacks
options.B: Call Sara to confirm her food allergies
options.C: Cancel the dinner because of allergy concerns
options.D: Ask Marco to bring extra wine bottles

questions.explanation: 女生最后说会handle the cheese run after class，所以会去商店买。B是话题相关但她没说要打电话。C完全相反。D是无关延伸。

items.situation: a student asking a shop about repairing a cracked phone screen

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, my phone screen cracked yesterday when I dropped it on the sidewalk. Can someone here fix it?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure, we do screen replacements. Is the phone still turning on?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: It is, and the touch still works. Just lots of cracks across the surface.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good. For your model, the replacement runs about sixty-five dollars and takes around two hours.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Two hours? I have class at three.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Then I'd suggest dropping it off in the morning. We open at eight, and your phone will be ready before your class.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll come back tomorrow at eight sharp.

questions.stem: What does the man tell the woman about the repair?

options.A: The phone needs new internal components
options.B: The repair will be free with student ID
options.C: The shop closes early on weekends only
options.D: The repair takes about two hours to complete

questions.explanation: 男生明确说大约需要两小时。A是凭空捏造的细节——只换屏幕。B完全没提。C是无关信息。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do tomorrow morning?

options.A: Take her phone to a different repair shop
options.B: Bring her phone to the shop when it opens at eight
options.C: Buy a brand-new phone instead of fixing this one
options.D: Skip her three o'clock class entirely

questions.explanation: 女生最后说明天八点回来，所以会在开门时把手机送过来。A是相反选择。C她已决定修不是换。D无关延伸。

items.situation: two friends sharing cooking tips while grocery shopping

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Wait, do you always pick the bunched broccoli over the pre-cut florets? I usually just grab the bag.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, the bunched ones last way longer in the fridge. Pre-cut florets start wilting in three days.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Huh. I've definitely had to toss a half-eaten bag.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Same with herbs. Buy them as a whole bunch, wrap them in a damp paper towel, and they'll stay fresh for a week.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Damp paper towel? I've been throwing herbs in a plastic bag and watching them go slimy.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Try it next time. Honestly changed my cooking.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'm definitely going to test that tonight.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: Practical tips for keeping produce fresh longer
options.B: The best supermarket to shop at near campus
options.C: A recipe one of them wants to try tonight
options.D: The cost of broccoli compared to other vegetables

questions.explanation: 对话核心是男生分享如何让蔬菜和香草保鲜更久。B完全没提。C借用了'tonight'做表面陷阱但跑题。D只是表层细节。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do tonight?

options.A: Throw out the herbs she bought last week
options.B: Switch to buying only canned vegetables in the future
options.C: Try wrapping her fresh herbs in a damp paper towel
options.D: Cook the broccoli before it has a chance to wilt

questions.explanation: 女生最后说今晚要试一下，指的是男生教的湿纸巾包香草的方法。A借用了她以前的做法。B过度推论。D是话题相关但不是她说要做的。

items.situation: a student asking a TA about a makeup chemistry quiz after being sick

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I missed the chemistry quiz last Thursday because I had the flu. Can I still take it?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure. Professor Lee handles makeups. She's holding office hours tomorrow from two until four.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, I have lab tomorrow afternoon. Is Friday possible instead?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Friday works. Just email her by tonight so she can pull a fresh copy.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Got it. Do I need a doctor's note for proof?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yep, bring it to her office along with your student ID.

questions.stem: What does the man tell the woman about Professor Lee's office hours?

options.A: They are scheduled tomorrow afternoon from two to four
options.B: They were cancelled because of the recent holiday
options.C: They have moved permanently to Friday afternoons
options.D: They are only available by appointment this week

questions.explanation: 男生明确说Professor Lee明天from two until four有办公室时间，所以A正确。B、C、D都没有在对话里出现，是话题相关但未被提及的干扰项。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Drop by Professor Lee's office tomorrow afternoon
options.B: Retake the entire chemistry course next semester
options.C: Email Professor Lee tonight and bring her note Friday
options.D: Ask another student to share the quiz answers

questions.explanation: 女生明天下午有lab所以不能去，男生建议Friday并要她今晚email，还要带医生证明，所以C正确。A被她自己排除（lab冲突）；B属于过度推断，没人提过重修；D完全不在对话中。

items.situation: two students discussing a botanical garden orchid exhibit they recently visited

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Did you finally make it to the botanical garden's orchid exhibit?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yes! Honestly, the rare cloud-forest section completely blew me away.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, I missed that part. I spent most of my time at the carnivorous plants display.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Really? Was it worth it? The online reviews were pretty mixed.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Definitely. They had a live pitcher plant demonstration every hour. It's not in the brochure though.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Wow, I have to go back. Did you grab a seedling kit at the gift shop?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: No, a friend told me those sold out by Sunday afternoon.

questions.stem: What did the man find most worthwhile at the exhibit?

options.A: The rare cloud-forest orchid display section
options.B: The botanical garden's online reviews and brochure
options.C: The hourly live pitcher plant demonstration
options.D: The seedling kits sold at the small gift shop

questions.explanation: 男生明说Carnivorous plants部分的pitcher plant demonstration绝对值得，所以C正确。A是女生最喜欢的部分，属misattributed；B只是被提到，男生没说有价值；D男生没买到，是相关但非他喜欢的。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Return to the garden to see the pitcher plant demonstration
options.B: Order a botanical seedling kit through an online store
options.C: Write a negative review based on the man's comments
options.D: Skip the orchid section on her next garden visit

questions.explanation: 女生说"I have to go back"，男生刚刚强烈推荐了pitcher plant demonstration，所以她最可能去看，A正确。B无依据；C与她的态度相反，过度推断；D她已经表达对orchid喜爱，与对话相反。

items.situation: a student asking the registrar about withdrawing from a class after the deadline

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I need to drop my statistics class. The deadline already passed, right?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, last Friday. Late withdrawals need a signed petition from your academic advisor.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Will it still show as a regular W on my transcript, or a failing grade?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: If approved, just a standard W with no GPA impact. If denied, the course stays.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Stressful. Can my advisor sign remotely? She's at a conference until Wednesday.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: She can email a scan, but the original signature must reach our office by month-end.

questions.stem: What is the woman mainly trying to do?

options.A: Switch her statistics section to a different professor
options.B: Withdraw from her statistics class after the official deadline
options.C: Argue that her low statistics grade is unfair this term
options.D: Confirm that her transcript has no failing grades listed

questions.explanation: 女生一开口就说"I need to drop my statistics class"，整段对话围绕逾期退课流程，所以B正确。A、C都不是对话内容；D只是顺带询问的细节，不是主要目的。

questions.stem: What does the man say about the advisor's signature requirement?

options.A: It must be delivered to the office only in person
options.B: A statement from a doctor can substitute for the signature
options.C: Permission from the statistics professor is also required
options.D: An emailed scan works, but the original is due by month-end

questions.explanation: 男生明确说She can email a scan，但the original signature must reach our office by month-end，所以D正确。A与对话相反（可以先发扫描件）；B、C都没在对话中提到，是相关但未被陈述。

items.situation: two friends coordinating a weekend hiking or beach trip

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, are we still on for the Cascade ridge hike this Saturday? I've been checking the weather and it's looking pretty rainy.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I saw that too. Honestly, the trail gets really slick when it's wet, and the view at the top is the whole point. Want to push it to next weekend instead?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That works for me. But maybe we just swap to the coastal walk this Saturday? It's covered most of the way and we don't waste the day.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, that's a great call. The coastal route has those cafés near the lighthouse. We can grab lunch halfway.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll text Sam too, so she can come if she wants. I'll meet you at the bus stop by nine.

questions.stem: What is the conversation mainly about?

options.A: How to change weekend plans because of bad hiking weather
options.B: Why the Cascade ridge trail closes during the autumn season
options.C: How the lighthouse café upgraded its lunch menu recently
options.D: Why Sam refused to join the coastal walk this Saturday

questions.explanation: 对话围绕因下雨更改原定登山计划展开，A 正确。B、C 凭空发挥；D 与对话中邀请 Sam 矛盾。

questions.stem: What will the speakers most likely do this Saturday?

options.A: Hike the Cascade ridge in the rain despite the conditions
options.B: Cancel all weekend plans and stay home in the dorms
options.C: Take the coastal walk and stop for lunch near the lighthouse
options.D: Drive to a different city to avoid the rainy weather entirely

questions.explanation: 对话最终敲定改走海岸路线并在灯塔附近吃午餐，故 C 对。A 与决定相反；B、D 与对话无依据。

items.situation: two students talking about which elective class to take next term

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'm trying to pick an elective for next term and I can't decide. Have you taken any good ones?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, the film analysis seminar with Professor Cho was the best class I've taken. Tons of writing but really small group.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm. I was looking at that one. Is the workload actually heavy though? I've got organic chem next term.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's manageable if you stay on top of the weekly responses. The screenings happen Thursday evenings, so plan your lab nights around that.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's a good warning. I'll check whether the Thursday evening lab block works first.

questions.stem: According to the man, what makes the film analysis seminar valuable?

options.A: It requires no writing assignments throughout the semester
options.B: It is taught by Professor Cho in a small group setting
options.C: It meets only twice a month for short discussions
options.D: It guarantees a high grade for all enrolled students

questions.explanation: 男生强调课程是 Professor Cho 教、小组小，故 B 对。A 与'tons of writing'相反；C、D 凭空发挥。

questions.stem: What will the woman likely do before enrolling?

options.A: Drop her planned organic chemistry course for next term
options.B: Wait until Professor Cho announces a different time slot
options.C: Speak with Professor Cho about reducing the workload
options.D: Check whether her Thursday evening lab schedule will work

questions.explanation: 女生在结尾明确说要先确认周四晚上的实验时间，故 D 对。A、B、C 都没有依据，且与她最后的发言不一致。

items.situation: two friends recommending a podcast or TV series to each other

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you finished that medical drama yet? The one with the rural clinic?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I burned through it last week. The first season is amazing, but I'd skip the second.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh really? What's wrong with the second?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: They added too many side characters and the pacing falls apart. If you want something better, try that podcast on long-haul truckers. It's only six episodes and totally absorbing.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, a podcast is honestly easier with my study schedule. Send me the link?

questions.stem: Why does the man advise skipping the second season of the drama?

options.A: Its first episode was poorly written
options.B: Its rural clinic setting was abandoned
options.C: It added too many side characters and the pacing breaks down
options.D: It was canceled before the season finale aired

questions.explanation: 男生直接说'They added too many side characters and the pacing falls apart'，故 C 对。A、B、D 都未提及。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do after the conversation?

options.A: Watch the entire second season of the medical drama
options.B: Recommend the drama to her organic chemistry classmates
options.C: Listen to the long-haul trucker podcast in her free time
options.D: Send the man a link to a different podcast she likes

questions.explanation: 女生说播客更适合自己的日程并要链接，故 C 对。A 与男生建议相反；B、D 没有依据。

items.situation: a student returning a too-small sweater at a store counter

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I bought this sweater here last week but it's a bit tight in the shoulders. Can I exchange it for a larger size?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure, do you have the receipt with you?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I have the digital one on my phone. Does that work?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's fine. Let me see if we still carry that color in a medium. Hmm, looks like the cream is sold out, but we have it in dark green and navy.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, the navy actually sounds better with what I wear. Let's go with that.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Great. I'll grab it from the back and we'll process the swap at the register.

questions.stem: Why is the woman returning the sweater?

options.A: Because the color faded after one wash
options.B: Because she lost the original paper receipt entirely
options.C: Because the cream color was discontinued by the brand
options.D: Because the sweater is too tight in the shoulders for her

questions.explanation: 女生明确说'a bit tight in the shoulders'，故 D 对。A、C 没有依据；B 与对话中她有电子收据相反。

questions.stem: What will most likely happen next?

options.A: The woman will leave with the same sweater unchanged
options.B: The man will refund her purchase entirely without exchange
options.C: The man will order the cream color from another store branch
options.D: The woman will receive a navy sweater after the swap at the register

questions.explanation: 对话结尾男生取一件海军蓝去收银台办理调换，故 D 对。A、B 与决定相反；C 凭空发挥。

items.situation: two students swapping music recommendations because one is bored of the same pop singers

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'm so bored of the same pop singers on my playlist. Any suggestions?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, you should try some indie folk. There's a Norwegian duo whose harmonies are unreal.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, folk isn't usually my thing. Is it the gentle acoustic kind?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Not really. They use a lot of layered synthesizer textures under the vocals, so it's more atmospheric than acoustic. The latest album has been on repeat for me.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that sounds interesting actually. Synthesizers with folk vocals could break me out of the loop.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, give it one full listen on the bus tomorrow.

questions.stem: What is the main point of the conversation?

options.A: The man is recommending atmospheric indie folk to the woman who is tired of pop
options.B: The woman is convincing the man to abandon his Norwegian duo entirely
options.C: The two friends are debating whether folk vocals should use synthesizers
options.D: The man wants the woman to stop using her commute for listening to music

questions.explanation: 男生向厌烦流行的女生推荐挪威组合的氛围民谣，A 概括最准确。B、C、D 与对话事实相反或凭空发挥。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do tomorrow?

options.A: Buy a vinyl copy of the album from a downtown store
options.B: Ask Sam for an alternative pop singer recommendation
options.C: Listen to the Norwegian duo's album during her bus ride
options.D: Take the man to a live concert by the same Norwegian duo

questions.explanation: 男生建议在公交上完整听一遍，女生表现出兴趣，故 C 对。A、B、D 都没有对话依据。

items.situation: two friends sharing cooking tips while grocery shopping

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Are you grabbing tomatoes for that pasta sauce you keep raving about? Mine never tastes right.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, but honestly the trick isn't the tomatoes. It's adding a pinch of sugar at the end to cut the acidity.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Sugar? Huh, I always thought you had to simmer it for hours.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It helps, but the sugar does most of the work. Oh, and grab the canned ones, not fresh. They're riper.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Wait, canned are actually better? That feels backwards.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: For sauce, totally. They're picked at peak ripeness. I'll text you the recipe with the amounts.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That'd be perfect. I'm definitely making this tonight.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: How to improve the woman's homemade pasta sauce.
options.B: Which grocery store has the freshest vegetables.
options.C: Why the man stopped cooking pasta at home.
options.D: How long it takes to simmer a tomato sauce.

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：对话核心是男生向女生分享让番茄酱更好吃的技巧（加糖、用罐装番茄），帮助她改进做不好的酱料。B选项错误，对话没有比较哪家超市蔬菜更新鲜。C选项错误，男生并没有停止在家做意面，反而在分享自己的做法。D选项错误，炖煮时间只是对话中的一个小细节，不是主旨。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do later?

options.A: Simmer her sauce for several hours as usual.
options.B: Ask the man to cook the pasta sauce for her.
options.C: Try making the sauce with canned tomatoes and sugar.
options.D: Switch to buying only fresh tomatoes from now on.

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：女生说她今晚一定要做（I'm definitely making this tonight），并接受了男生关于用罐装番茄和加糖的建议，所以她最可能按新方法尝试。A选项错误，男生说糖才是关键，长时间炖煮不是重点，女生也不再坚持老办法。B选项错误，对话没有提到让男生替她做。D选项错误，男生建议做酱时用罐装而非新鲜番茄，所以只买新鲜番茄与对话相反。

items.situation: two students swapping music recommendations because one is bored of the same pop singers

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, I'm so bored of my playlist. It's the same three pop singers on repeat.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, you should check out this jazz trio I've been into. They're super relaxing for studying.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Jazz? I've never really listened to it. Isn't it kind of old-fashioned?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Not these guys. They mix in electronic beats, so it feels modern. I'll send you their album link.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, that actually sounds cool. Send it over.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Done. Start with the third track. It's the one that got me hooked.

questions.stem: What kind of music does the man recommend?

options.A: Classical piano music.
options.B: A jazz trio with electronic beats.
options.C: The same pop singers she already likes.
options.D: Old-fashioned big-band swing.

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：男生说他推荐的是一个融合电子节拍的爵士三重奏（this jazz trio... they mix in electronic beats）。A选项错误，对话没有提到古典钢琴。C选项错误，那些流行歌手正是女生听腻了的，不是男生的推荐。D选项错误，男生特意强调这些音乐不是老派的，所以老式大乐队摇摆乐与对话相反。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Delete all the pop songs from her playlist.
options.B: Ask the man to make her a new playlist.
options.C: Go to a live jazz concert with the man.
options.D: Listen to the album the man sent her.

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：女生说听起来很酷、让男生发过来（that actually sounds cool. Send it over），男生发了专辑链接并建议先听第三首，所以她最可能去听这张专辑。A选项错误，她只是听腻了，没说要删掉流行歌曲。B选项错误，男生只是发链接，没说要替她做歌单。C选项错误，对话没有提到去看现场演出。

items.situation: two roommates working out a fair quiet-hours schedule

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Can we talk about quiet hours? I've got an early biology lab, so I really need to sleep by eleven.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, no worries. The thing is, I usually do my gaming calls late at night. How about I just use headphones after ten?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That would help a ton, actually.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: And you can have the room to yourself in the early mornings since I sleep in. Fair trade?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Totally fair. Should we write it down so we don't forget?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good idea. I'll stick a note on the fridge with the agreement.

questions.stem: Why does the woman start the conversation?

options.A: To complain that the man games too much.
options.B: To ask the man to switch dorm rooms with her.
options.C: To set up a quiet-hours arrangement with her roommate.
options.D: To borrow the man's headphones for her lab.

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：女生开口是想商量安静时段（Can we talk about quiet hours?），因为她有早课需要早睡，最终两人达成了协议。A选项错误，她没有抱怨男生玩游戏太多，而是友好地协商。B选项错误，对话没有提到换宿舍。D选项错误，是男生提出自己戴耳机，女生并不是来借耳机的。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do at night from now on?

options.A: Wear headphones during his late gaming calls.
options.B: Stop making gaming calls entirely.
options.C: Move his gaming to the dorm common room.
options.D: Wake up early to game in the mornings.

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：男生提议十点后自己戴耳机（I just use headphones after ten），女生表示这很有帮助，所以他晚上打游戏时最可能戴耳机。B选项错误，他没说要完全停止打游戏，只是戴耳机。C选项错误，对话没有提到去公共休息室。D选项错误，男生说自己会睡懒觉，把清晨留给女生，并非早起打游戏。

items.situation: two friends planning a birthday surprise for a classmate

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So Daniel's birthday is Friday. I was thinking we surprise him after his afternoon seminar.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Love it. Should we do it in the lounge? There's space for everyone there.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll handle the decorations and the cake.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Great. Then I'll text the rest of the group so everyone knows to hide by four.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, and let's keep it quiet around him. He has no idea, right?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: None at all. I'll message everyone the second I leave here.

questions.stem: Where will the surprise party take place?

options.A: Outside Daniel's seminar room.
options.B: At a nearby cafe.
options.C: In Daniel's dorm room.
options.D: In the student lounge.

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：男生提议在休息室办（Should we do it in the lounge?），女生回答很好，所以惊喜派对会在学生休息室举行。A选项错误，对话只提到在他研讨课后给他惊喜，地点是休息室，不是教室门外。B选项错误，对话没有提到咖啡馆。C选项错误，对话没有提到在宿舍。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do after the conversation?

options.A: Buy the birthday cake himself.
options.B: Message the group about hiding by four.
options.C: Tell Daniel about the surprise party.
options.D: Decorate the lounge for the party.

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：男生说他一离开就给大家发消息（I'll message everyone the second I leave here），要通知大家四点前藏好，所以他最可能去给小组发消息。A选项错误，女生说蛋糕由她负责。C选项错误，他们想保密，男生说Daniel完全不知情，不会告诉他。D选项错误，布置和蛋糕都由女生负责。

items.situation: two friends discussing a movie one just saw — good twists but too long

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I finally saw that detective movie everyone's talking about. The twist at the end completely fooled me.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh nice, so you'd recommend it? I've been on the fence.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Mostly, yeah. My only complaint is that it drags in the middle. It's almost three hours long.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Three hours? Yikes. That's a big commitment for a weeknight.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right. If you go, save it for the weekend when you're not tired.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good call. I'll plan to catch it Saturday afternoon then.

questions.stem: What complaint does the woman have about the movie?

options.A: It is too long and drags in the middle.
options.B: The ending twist was easy to predict.
options.C: The detective plot was hard to follow.
options.D: The ticket was too expensive.

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：女生说她唯一的不满是中段拖沓、电影近三个小时长（My only complaint is that it drags in the middle. It's almost three hours long），所以她唯一的不满是电影太长、中段拖沓。B选项错误，她说结局的反转把她完全骗到了，所以反转并不容易猜。C选项错误，对话没有说剧情难懂。D选项错误，对话没有提到票价。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do?

options.A: Skip the movie because it is too long.
options.B: Watch the movie on a weeknight after class.
options.C: See the movie on Saturday afternoon.
options.D: Wait for the movie to come out online.

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：男生说他打算周六下午去看（I'll plan to catch it Saturday afternoon then），采纳了女生周末看的建议，所以他最可能周六下午去看。A选项错误，他没有放弃，而是打算周末去看。B选项错误，女生建议别在疲惫的工作日晚上看，男生也同意周末看，所以工作日晚上被否决了。D选项错误，对话没有提到等线上版本。

items.situation: two students talking about a pet or houseplant one is caring for

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, I'm watching my roommate's cat this week while she's at the conference. But I think something's wrong with him.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh no. What's he doing?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: He keeps ignoring his food. I've filled the bowl twice and he barely touches it. He just sits by the window.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm. Did your roommate leave instructions?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: She left a note, but I can't read her handwriting. It might say something about wet food.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Why don't you just text her a photo of the note? She can tell you exactly what to do.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's a good call. I'll send it right now.

questions.stem: What problem is the woman having with the cat?

options.A: The cat is not eating its food.
options.B: The cat scratched the furniture.
options.C: The cat ran away from the apartment.
options.D: The cat is afraid of the window.

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：女生明确说猫一直不碰食物（keeps ignoring his food, barely touches it），这是她担心的核心问题。B选项错误，对话完全没有提到抓家具。C选项错误，猫并没有跑掉，它就坐在窗边。D选项错误，猫坐在窗边并不是害怕窗户，只是没吃东西，这是对细节的曲解。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Take the cat to a veterinarian immediately.
options.B: Buy a different brand of cat food.
options.C: Send her roommate a photo of the note.
options.D: Wait until her roommate comes home.

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：男生建议把字条拍照发给室友，女生回答"that's a good call. I'll send it right now"，所以她接下来会发字条照片。A选项过度推断，对话中没人提到去看兽医。B选项错误，换猫粮只是男生的猜测，女生并没有决定这么做。D选项错误，女生急于现在就解决，并没有说要等室友回来。

items.situation: two friends figuring out a bus or train schedule for a day trip

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So for Saturday's trip to the coast, I checked and there's a train at eight and another at ten. Which should we take?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The eight o'clock one. The ten gets us there too late to do anything.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay. Oh wait, the eight only runs on weekdays. On Saturdays the first one is actually nine fifteen.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Seriously? That's annoying. Well, nine fifteen still beats ten.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right. And return trains leave every hour until eight, so we don't have to rush back.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect. Let's book the nine fifteen. I'll grab the tickets online so we don't have to line up.

questions.stem: Which train will the friends take on Saturday?

options.A: The eight o'clock train.
options.B: The nine fifteen train.
options.C: The ten o'clock train.
options.D: The last train of the day.

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：女生指出周六最早的车是九点十五（first one is actually nine fifteen），男生说"Let's book the nine fifteen"，所以他们坐九点十五的车。A选项错误，八点的车只在工作日开，周六没有。C选项错误，男生明确说十点太晚了。D选项错误，最后一班是傍晚的返程车，不是他们出发要坐的车，这是对信息的张冠李戴。

questions.stem: What does the man imply he will do?

options.A: Drive to the coast instead of taking the train.
options.B: Wait to buy tickets at the station on Saturday.
options.C: Cancel the trip because of the schedule change.
options.D: Purchase the tickets ahead of time online.

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：男生说"I'll grab the tickets online so we don't have to line up"，暗示他会提前在网上买票。A选项无依据，全程没有提到开车。B选项与原文相反，他正是为了不排队才提前网购。C选项过度推断，时刻表变了但两人照常出行，没人提取消。

items.situation: two friends debating whether a new cafe is worth the price

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you tried that new cafe on Fifth Street? Everyone's posting about it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I went yesterday. The coffee's amazing, honestly. But a latte is six dollars.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Six dollars? Wow. That's almost double the place by campus.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. The drinks are great, but I can't afford it more than once in a while.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm. Maybe it's a treat-yourself kind of spot, not an everyday thing.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's a good way to put it. I'd go again for a special occasion, just not on a regular Tuesday.

questions.stem: What does the man say about the new cafe?

options.A: The coffee tastes terrible.
options.B: It is always too crowded to sit.
options.C: The coffee is good but expensive.
options.D: It is closer than the campus cafe.

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：男生说咖啡"amazing"但一杯拿铁要六美元（好喝但贵），这正是他的评价。A选项与原文相反，他说咖啡很棒。B选项无依据，对话没提拥挤或没座位。D选项错误，女生说这家几乎是校园那家价格的两倍，而且没有比较距离，是对细节的曲解。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about how the man will use the cafe?

options.A: He will go there only on special occasions.
options.B: He will go there every morning before class.
options.C: He will stop drinking coffee altogether.
options.D: He will ask the cafe to lower its prices.

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：男生说"I'd go again for a special occasion, just not on a regular Tuesday"，暗示他只会偶尔在特殊场合去。B选项与原文相反，他明确说不会平常日子常去。C选项过度推断，他只是少去这家贵店，没说要戒咖啡。D选项无依据，没人提让店家降价。

items.situation: two students chatting about a part-time job one just started at a factory

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I heard you started that part-time job at the packaging factory. How's it going so far?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's okay, but the schedule's killing me. My shift starts at five in the morning.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Five? Yikes. How do you even make your nine o'clock lecture?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's the thing, I barely do. I'm exhausted by the time class starts, and I keep nodding off.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That doesn't sound sustainable. Can you switch to a later shift?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Maybe. My supervisor mentioned there might be afternoon openings next month. I should probably ask about it.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Definitely. Your grades matter more than a few extra early shifts.

questions.stem: What is the main topic of the conversation?

options.A: How to find a part-time job near campus.
options.B: Why the man decided to quit his job.
options.C: Which lecture the man finds most difficult.
options.D: The problem the man's work schedule is causing.

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：对话核心是男生五点的早班让他疲惫、上课打瞌睡，影响学习，这是贯穿始终的问题。A选项错误，男生已经有工作了，不是在找工作。B选项与情况相反，他没决定辞职，而是考虑换班次。C选项太狭窄，打瞌睡只是问题的一个表现，且并非某门课特别难。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do?

options.A: Drop his nine o'clock lecture.
options.B: Ask his supervisor about an afternoon shift.
options.C: Quit the factory job right away.
options.D: Start going to bed much earlier each night.

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：男生说主管提到下个月可能有下午的空缺，"I should probably ask about it"，所以他很可能去问换下午班。A选项过度推断，他想解决的是上课打瞌睡，没说要退课。C选项与原文相反，他考虑的是换班次而非立刻辞职。D选项无依据，对话里没提早点睡这个方案。

items.situation: two students planning what cheese and food to buy for a dinner party

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, for Saturday's dinner party, I'm thinking we do a cheese board to start. Maybe three kinds?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sounds great. Just remember Priya's coming, and she can't do anything too strong-smelling. The blue cheese last time was a disaster.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh right, I forgot. So let's skip the blue. A soft brie, a sharp cheddar, and something milder?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect. And crackers, obviously. I'll handle those and grab some grapes too.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Great. I'll get the cheeses on my way home, since the good shop is right by my bus stop.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Deal. Oh, and don't forget honey for the brie. Trust me, it makes a difference.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly trying to decide?

options.A: What food to prepare for their dinner party.
options.B: Whether to invite Priya to the party.
options.C: Which grocery store has the lowest prices.
options.D: How to make a blue cheese dish for guests.

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：整段对话都在商量晚宴前的食物——选哪几种奶酪、配饼干、葡萄和蜂蜜，这是主旨。B选项错误，Priya已经确定要来，他们只是考虑她的口味，不是讨论是否邀请。C选项无依据，没有比较哪家店便宜。D选项与情况相反，他们专门决定不做蓝纹奶酪，因为上次很糟糕。

questions.stem: Why will the speakers avoid blue cheese this time?

options.A: It is the most expensive cheese available.
options.B: The shop near the bus stop is out of it.
options.C: One guest dislikes strong-smelling foods.
options.D: Neither of them knows how to serve it.

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：男生提到Priya受不了气味太重的东西，上次的蓝纹奶酪是"a disaster"，可推断他们为了照顾这位客人而避开蓝纹奶酪。A选项无依据，对话没提价格。B选项无依据，店里是否有货从未提及。D选项错误，他们不做蓝纹是因为客人口味，不是不会摆盘，属于编造的理由。

items.situation: two students chatting about a part-time job one just started at a warehouse

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, you started that warehouse packing job, right? How's it going?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, it's exhausting. I'm on my feet for eight hours, and the shifts are mostly late at night.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Late nights? Doesn't that wreck your class schedule?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's the thing. I keep dozing off in my morning lecture, and my grades are starting to slip.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That sounds rough. Maybe ask your supervisor about switching to weekend shifts?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I think I have to. The money's good, but it's not worth failing a course over.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: The drawbacks of the man's new job
options.B: How to find a part-time job on campus
options.C: The man's decision to drop a course
options.D: A disagreement about working night shifts

questions.explanation: 对话围绕这份工作带来的疲惫与影响成绩等弊端，A 正确。B 找工作未谈；C 他只是担心挂科并非决定退课；D 两人并无争执。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do?

options.A: Quit the job right away
options.B: Stop going to his morning lecture
options.C: Ask to change his work shifts
options.D: Hire a tutor for the course

questions.explanation: 他认同要找主管换成周末班，C 正确。A 立刻辞职过度推断；B 是问题不是打算；D 请家教未提。

items.situation: two friends sharing cooking tips while grocery shopping

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: These tomatoes look great. I'm finally going to try making that pasta sauce.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, nice! Quick tip: add a pinch of sugar to balance the acidity. It makes a huge difference.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sugar? In a tomato sauce? I never would've guessed.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Trust me. And let it simmer on low for at least an hour. Don't rush it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: An hour, okay. I usually get impatient and crank up the heat.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's the mistake. High heat turns it bitter. Low and slow is the real secret.

questions.stem: What does the woman suggest adding to the sauce?

options.A: A handful of fresh basil
options.B: A small amount of sugar
options.C: A splash of olive oil
options.D: A pinch of black pepper

questions.explanation: 她建议加少许糖来中和酸味，B 正确。A、C 罗勒和橄榄油未提及；D 黑胡椒是改动的细节。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do differently this time?

options.A: Buy a jar of canned sauce instead
options.B: Add even more tomatoes
options.C: Skip the simmering step entirely
options.D: Cook the sauce over low heat

questions.explanation: 他过去爱开大火，现在会改用小火慢炖，D 正确。A 买现成酱无关；B 加更多番茄未提；C 跳过慢炖与建议相反。

items.situation: two students chatting about an art class one just signed up for

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I just signed up for that weekend pottery class at the community center.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Pottery? I took the watercolor one there last term. Did they make you buy your own supplies?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's exactly what I wanted to ask. The flyer wasn't clear about it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: For watercolor, the basic materials were included, but you paid extra for the nicer brushes.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Good to know. I'll bring some cash in case the clay tools cost extra.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Smart. Email the instructor too. They answered all my questions really fast.

questions.stem: What class did the man take previously?

options.A: A pottery class
options.B: A gardening class
options.C: A watercolor painting class
options.D: A photography class

questions.explanation: 男生说上学期上过水彩课，C 正确。A 陶艺是女生报的；B、D 园艺和摄影都未提及。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: Bring extra money to the class
options.B: Cancel her class registration
options.C: Buy expensive brushes in advance
options.D: Switch into the watercolor class

questions.explanation: 她打算带些现金以防工具另收费，A 正确。B 取消报名过度推断；C 买贵画笔是水彩课的情况；D 换课无依据。

items.situation: two friends deciding whether to buy concert tickets now or wait

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Did you see the band we both like is playing here next month?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yes! But the tickets are kind of pricey right now. Should we wait a bit?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I've heard prices sometimes drop closer to the date, if a show doesn't sell out.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: True, but this band is really popular. It could easily sell out within days.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good point. I'd rather pay a little more than miss it completely.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Same here. Let's just grab them tonight before they're gone.

questions.stem: Why is the woman hesitant to buy the tickets right now?

options.A: She is not free on that night
options.B: She does not really like the band
options.C: She has already seen them perform
options.D: The tickets are expensive at the moment

questions.explanation: 她说现在票价偏贵，故而犹豫，D 正确。A 时间冲突未提；B 与两人都喜欢相反；C 看过演出未提。

questions.stem: What will the speakers most likely do?

options.A: Wait until the prices drop later
options.B: Buy the tickets tonight
options.C: Look for a different concert
options.D: Ask other friends to buy them

questions.explanation: 两人决定今晚就买，B 正确。A 等降价被否决；C 换演出无依据；D 让别人买是无关步骤。

items.situation: two students comparing how they get to campus

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You bike to campus every day, right? Isn't that exhausting?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Not really. It's faster than the bus, and I skip the crowded stops.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I take the bus, but lately it's always packed and running late.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's why I switched. Though when it rains, I admit I really miss the dry bus.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yeah, the weather's the catch. Maybe I'll try walking on nice days.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Walking's great if you have the time. It's about thirty minutes from the dorms.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly talking about?

options.A: Different ways of getting to campus
options.B: A problem with the campus bus schedule
options.C: How to stay fit while commuting
options.D: Why the man dislikes biking

questions.explanation: 两人比较骑车、坐公交、步行等通勤方式，A 正确。B 公交只是其一，过窄；C 健身偏题；D 与他喜欢骑车相反。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do on a sunny day?

options.A: Buy a bicycle of her own
options.B: Keep taking the crowded bus
options.C: Try walking to campus
options.D: Ask the man for a ride

questions.explanation: 她说天气好时想试着步行，C 正确。A 买自行车过度推断；B 继续坐拥挤公交被她想避开；D 搭车无依据。

items.situation: Two students discuss a new restaurant downtown and which dish to order.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, did you try that new Thai place downtown? Everyone's talking about it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I went last week! Honestly, the green curry was good, but the mango sticky rice is what you really go for. It's incredible.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Really? I usually skip dessert. Is it that special?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Trust me. The rice is warm, the mango's super ripe. I'd go back just for that, even though it's a bit pricey.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, okay. I'm meeting Lena there Friday, so I'll have to convince her to save room.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good call. Just don't fill up on the appetizers first.

questions.stem: Which dish does the man recommend most highly?

options.A: The mango sticky rice
options.B: The green curry
options.C: The appetizer platter
options.D: The Thai iced tea

questions.explanation: 男生明确说芒果糯米饭才是真正值得去吃的（what you really go for），是他最推荐的菜。B绿咖喱他只说good，并非最推荐，属于wrong_detail。C开胃菜他反而提醒不要吃太多，属于misattributed。D红茶对话中根本没提到，属于related_but_not_stated。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do on Friday?

options.A: Cancel her dinner plans with Lena
options.B: Order only appetizers to save money
options.C: Try the dessert the man suggested
options.D: Eat at a different restaurant instead

questions.explanation: 女生原本不吃甜点，但被男生说服后表示要让Lena也留肚子（save room），暗示她打算尝尝那道甜点，所以C正确。A取消计划与对话相反，属于over_inference。B她反而被提醒别吃太多开胃菜，属于rejected_option。D换餐厅没有任何依据，属于unrelated_next_step。

items.situation: Two roommates work out a fair cleaning schedule for shared spaces.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: We really need a plan for the kitchen. It's getting out of hand, and I keep ending up cleaning it alone.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, sorry about that. My class schedule's been brutal. How about we just split it by week? I take this week, you take next?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, a whole week feels like too much at once. What if we each take specific days instead?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, that's smarter. Okay, I'll do Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays. You take the other days?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Deal. And let's put it on a sticky note on the fridge so nobody forgets.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll write it up tonight.

questions.stem: How do the roommates decide to divide the cleaning?

options.A: By alternating full weeks
options.B: By assigning specific days to each person
options.C: By hiring a cleaning service
options.D: By cleaning together every evening

questions.explanation: 两人最终决定按具体的星期几分工，男生负责周一三五，女生负责其余几天，所以B正确。A整周轮换是男生最初的提议，被女生否决了，属于rejected的wrong_detail。C请清洁服务对话从未提及，属于related_but_not_stated。D每晚一起打扫与他们分日子的方案相反，属于reversed_situation。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do tonight?

options.A: Clean the kitchen by himself
options.B: Ask for a new roommate
options.C: Complain to the dorm manager
options.D: Write the schedule on a note for the fridge

questions.explanation: 男生说今晚会把分工写下来（I'll write it up tonight），而女生提议贴在冰箱上的便利贴，所以D正确。A今晚打扫厨房不是他答应的事，属于unrelated_next_step。B换室友过于极端，属于over_inference。C向宿管投诉对话毫无依据，属于unrelated_next_step。

items.situation: Two friends recommend a documentary series to each other.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You finished that nature documentary series, right? The one about deep oceans?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I did, and honestly, I was skeptical at first. I figured it'd just be pretty footage with no real substance.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: And? Was it actually any good?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It surprised me. The science was solid, and each episode built on the last one. By the end I felt like I'd actually learned something.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Wow. I've got a long train ride home this weekend. Maybe I'll download it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Do it. Just start from episode one, though. They really build on each other.

questions.stem: What is the man mainly saying about the documentary series?

options.A: It has beautiful footage but little real content
options.B: It is too scientific for casual viewers
options.C: It was better and more informative than he expected
options.D: It would be good for a long train ride

questions.explanation: 男生主旨是这部纪录片超出他的预期，内容扎实、让他真正学到东西，所以C正确。A漂亮画面没内容正是他原本的怀疑，后来被推翻，属于reversed_situation。B太偏科学难懂对话没说，且与他的赞赏相反，属于wrong_topic。D适合长途火车是女生的想法，且只是一个细节，并非男生对纪录片的总体评价，属于too_narrow。

questions.stem: What does the man suggest the woman do?

options.A: Watch the episodes in order from the beginning
options.B: Skip ahead to the most exciting episode
options.C: Watch it only after reading reviews
options.D: Find a shorter series for the train ride

questions.explanation: 男生强调要从第一集开始按顺序看，因为各集层层递进，所以A正确。B跳着看与他的建议相反，属于rejected_option。C看影评后再看对话没提，属于unrelated_next_step。D找更短的剧集没有依据，属于unrelated_next_step。

items.situation: Two students compare how they get to campus each morning.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Do you take the bus to campus? I always see you near the stop.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Actually, no. I just walk. It's only fifteen minutes from my place, and it wakes me up.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Wow, lucky. I'm way too far to walk, so I bike. It's fast, but it's freezing this time of year.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yikes, biking in the cold sounds rough. Why not take the bus then?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: It's always packed in the morning, and it takes forever with all the stops.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Fair enough. Maybe just bundle up more until spring.

questions.stem: How does the man usually get to campus?

options.A: By bus
options.B: By bike
options.C: By car
options.D: On foot

questions.explanation: 男生说他就是走路去（I just walk），只要十五分钟，所以D正确。A坐公交是女生看到他在车站附近的误会，他明确否认了，属于wrong_detail。B骑车是女生的方式，属于misattributed。C开车对话从未提及，属于related_but_not_stated。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely keep doing to get to campus?

options.A: Start taking the crowded bus
options.B: Keep biking but dress more warmly
options.C: Begin walking like the man
options.D: Ask a friend for a ride each day

questions.explanation: 女生抱怨公交又挤又慢，男生建议她在春天前多穿点保暖，暗示她会继续骑车只是穿暖些，所以B正确。A改坐公交与她对公交的抱怨相反，属于rejected_option。C像男生一样走路她说自己住得太远走不了，属于rejected_option。D每天求人搭车对话毫无依据，属于unrelated_next_step。

items.situation: two students chatting about a part-time job one just started at a factory

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, I heard you started that part-time job at the chocolate factory. How's it going?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's good, actually! But my shift starts at five in the morning, so I'm always exhausted by afternoon.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Five a.m.? Yikes. That's brutal with classes too.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, the early start is rough. The pay's great, though, and they let me leave by ten, so I still make my lectures.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, well, if you're that tired, maybe you should drop your eight-thirty seminar and take the evening section instead.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: You know, that's a really good idea. I'll talk to my advisor about switching this week.

questions.stem: What does the man say is difficult about his new job?

options.A: His shift begins very early in the morning
options.B: The factory is far from campus
options.C: The pay is lower than he expected
options.D: He often has to work late at night

questions.explanation: 男士明确说他的班次早上五点开始，所以总是很累，A正确。B距离从未被提及，属于related_but_not_stated。C与对话相反，男士说工资很高，属于wrong_detail。D把时间弄反了，他是早班并十点前下班，属于wrong_detail。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do this week?

options.A: Quit his job at the factory
options.B: Ask for a later shift at work
options.C: Talk to his advisor about changing a class
options.D: Sign up for an extra morning seminar

questions.explanation: 男士最后说要和导师谈谈换到晚间班次的课，C正确。A过度推断，他没说要辞职且说工资很好，属于over_inference。B无对话依据，他没打算改班次而是改课，属于unrelated_next_step。D与情境相反，他想避开早上的课，属于rejected_option。

items.situation: two roommates working out a fair chore and quiet-hours schedule

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So now that we're rooming together, I think we should sort out chores and a quiet-hours rule before it becomes a problem.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good call. I'm a night owl, so I usually study till about one a.m. Does that bother you?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: A little. I have early labs, so I'd love lights out by midnight. Could you use the lounge after that?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure, no worries. I'll move to the lounge after twelve. And for chores, want to just alternate weeks?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll take this week's cleaning, you take next. Let's write it on the whiteboard so we don't forget.

questions.stem: What are the two roommates mainly discussing?

options.A: Whether to find a different apartment
options.B: How to set up rules for living together
options.C: Which classes they should take this term
options.D: A disagreement about a shared electricity bill

questions.explanation: 整段对话围绕安排家务和安静时间的规则，B正确。A情境相反，他们是在协商同住而非搬走，属于reversed_situation。C从未提及，属于wrong_topic。D账单话题完全没出现，属于wrong_topic。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do after midnight on study nights?

options.A: Go to sleep at the same time as the woman
options.B: Ask the woman to study somewhere else
options.C: Stop studying so late altogether
options.D: Study in the lounge instead of the room

questions.explanation: 男士同意午夜后去休息室学习，D正确。A与他夜猫子习惯不符且无依据，属于unrelated_next_step。B情境相反，是他自己让步去别处，属于reversed_situation。C过度推断，他只是换地点而非不再熬夜，属于over_inference。

items.situation: two students figuring out a time to meet for a group project

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: We really need to meet about the history project. When are you free this week?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm, Monday and Tuesday are packed with labs. How about Wednesday afternoon?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Wednesday I have work until six. Could we do it then, like six-thirty?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Actually, the library closes early on Wednesdays. Let's just meet Thursday at three instead.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Thursday at three works great. I'll book a study room and message the others.

questions.stem: When do the students finally agree to meet?

options.A: Monday morning
options.B: Wednesday evening
options.C: Thursday afternoon
options.D: Tuesday during labs

questions.explanation: 两人最终定在周四下午三点，C正确。A周一被排除因为有实验课，属于wrong_detail。B周三晚被否决因为图书馆早关门，属于wrong_detail。D周二也满是实验课，属于wrong_detail。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Reserve a study room and contact the group
options.B: Cancel her Wednesday work shift
options.C: Ask the professor for an extension
options.D: Finish the project entirely on her own

questions.explanation: 女士说她会订一个学习室并通知其他人，A正确。B无依据，她没说要请假，属于unrelated_next_step。C延期话题从未出现，属于unrelated_next_step。D过度推断，这是小组项目，她没打算独自完成，属于over_inference。

items.situation: a student returning a too-small sweater at a store counter

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I bought this wool sweater last weekend, but it's a medium and it's way too tight on me.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: No problem at all. Do you have your receipt with you?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I do, right here. I was hoping to exchange it for a large in the same color.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me check the stock. Hmm, we're out of large in navy here, but our downtown branch has two in stock.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh. Could you have one shipped here instead? I'd rather not make the trip downtown.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Of course. I'll order it to this store, and we'll text you when it arrives, usually within three days.

questions.stem: Why does the woman come to the store?

options.A: To ask for a full cash refund
options.B: To buy a sweater as a gift
options.C: To complain about poor stitching
options.D: To exchange a sweater for a bigger size

questions.explanation: 女士因毛衣太小想换成大号，D正确。A情境相反，她要的是换货不是退款，属于reversed_situation。B从未提及买礼物，属于wrong_topic。C她没抱怨质量问题，只是尺码太小，属于wrong_topic。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do after this conversation?

options.A: Drive to the downtown branch herself
options.B: Wait for a text that the sweater has arrived
options.C: Choose a different color of sweater
options.D: Return the sweater for store credit

questions.explanation: 店员说会把货订到这家店并发短信通知，女士同意，所以她会等短信，B正确。A情境相反，她明确说不想去市区，属于rejected_option。C她坚持要同色，属于rejected_option。D她要换货而非退积分，属于over_inference。

items.situation: two students planning what cheese and food to buy for a dinner party

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, for Saturday's dinner I want a really nice cheese board as the centerpiece. Any ideas?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Well, a sharp cheddar and a creamy brie usually please everyone. But remember Dana's lactose intolerant.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, right! I totally forgot. So we'd need something dairy-free for her too.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. There's a cashew-based cheese at the co-op that's surprisingly good. We could add some olives and crackers around it.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect. So cheddar, brie, the cashew one, plus olives and crackers. That covers everybody.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sounds great. The co-op closes at eight, so let's swing by right after our four o'clock class.

questions.stem: What is the conversation mainly about?

options.A: Deciding what foods to serve at a dinner party
options.B: Choosing a restaurant for Saturday night
options.C: Planning the guest list for a party
options.D: Learning how to make cheese at home

questions.explanation: 两人整段在商量晚宴要买的奶酪和配菜，A正确。B情境相反，他们是在家办晚宴而非去餐厅，属于reversed_situation。C只提到一位客人但重点是食物，属于too_narrow。D从未谈到自制奶酪，属于wrong_topic。

questions.stem: Why do they decide to include the cashew-based cheese?

options.A: Because it is the cheapest option at the co-op
options.B: Because the man prefers it to cheddar
options.C: So that a guest who can't eat dairy has a choice
options.D: Because the other cheeses were sold out

questions.explanation: 他们想到Dana乳糖不耐受，需要无乳制品的选择，所以加坚果奶酪，C正确。A价格从未提及，属于unrelated_next_step。B男士没说更喜欢它，属于over_inference。D其他奶酪并非售罄，属于wrong_detail。

items.situation: two friends coordinating a weekend hiking trip

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, are you still up for hiking Pine Ridge on Saturday? I checked the forecast and it's supposed to be sunny.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Definitely. But I heard the main trailhead lot fills up by eight, so we'd have to leave really early.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh no, eight? I was hoping to sleep in a little.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Well, there's a shuttle from the rec center that runs all morning. We could just take that instead of driving.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That sounds great, actually. No parking stress. What time does the first one leave?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Around nine, I think. Let me double-check the rec center website tonight and text you the schedule.

questions.stem: What are the two speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: How to get to a hiking trail on the weekend
options.B: Whether the weekend weather will be good enough to hike
options.C: Which hiking trail is the most challenging one nearby
options.D: How to sign up for a guided hiking tour

questions.explanation: 对话主线是周六去 Pine Ridge 徒步以及如何到达——是开车赶早占车位还是坐 rec center 的班车，故 A 正确。B 是 too_narrow：天气只在开头一句被顺带提到，不是核心话题。C 是 wrong_topic：从未比较过哪条路线最难。D 是 wrong_topic：没有任何关于报名向导团的内容。

questions.stem: What will the speakers most likely do on Saturday?

options.A: Arrive at the trailhead lot before eight o'clock
options.B: Cancel the trip if the parking lot is full
options.C: Take the rec center shuttle to the trail
options.D: Drive separately and meet at the summit

questions.explanation: 女生对班车方案说 'That sounds great'，男生还要发班车时刻表，可推断他们会坐班车，故 C 正确。A 是 rejected_option：女生不想那么早，两人转而选择班车，所以早于八点到停车场被否决了。B 是 over_inference：没人提到要取消行程。D 是 unrelated_next_step：对话中没有分开开车或在山顶碰面的任何依据。

items.situation: two students planning what cheese and food to buy for a dinner party

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So for the dinner party, I'm thinking we set out a cheese board before the main course. What should we grab?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let's get a soft brie and a sharp cheddar. Oh, and some crackers to go with them.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Good call. Should we add grapes too? They always look nice on the board.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure, grapes work. But skip the blue cheese this time—remember, Tom can't stand the smell.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right, I forgot about that. Okay, brie, cheddar, crackers, and grapes. I'll stop by the market after my last class.

questions.stem: Which item do the speakers decide NOT to buy?

options.A: Crackers for the cheese board
options.B: Blue cheese
options.C: A sharp cheddar
options.D: Grapes for decoration

questions.explanation: 男生明确说 'skip the blue cheese this time'，因为 Tom 受不了它的气味，故 B 是他们决定不买的，正确。A、C、D 都被明确列入购买清单（饼干、切达、葡萄），所以都是 wrong_detail：把要买的说成不买的。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Ask Tom which cheeses he prefers
options.B: Cancel the dinner party
options.C: Borrow a cheese board from a neighbor
options.D: Go to the market to buy the food

questions.explanation: 女生最后说 'I'll stop by the market after my last class'，可推断她接下来会去市场买东西，故 D 正确。A 是 unrelated_next_step：他们已替 Tom 做了决定，没说要再问他。B 是 over_inference：完全没有取消聚会的迹象。C 是 unrelated_next_step：对话从未提到借奶酪拼盘。

items.situation: two students chatting about a gardening class one of them took

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I just got back from that weekend gardening class at the campus greenhouse. It was so much fun.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh nice! What did you actually do there?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: We each potted our own herb garden—basil, mint, and parsley. They let us take the pots home.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's a great deal. Was it hard to get a spot? I've heard those fill up fast.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, yeah. I signed up the day registration opened. You should grab a spot early if you want in next month.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'll definitely do that. I've been wanting to grow something on my windowsill.

questions.stem: What did the woman do in the gardening class?

options.A: She learned to design a flower garden
options.B: She watched a demonstration on composting
options.C: She potted her own herb garden to take home
options.D: She helped plant trees around the greenhouse

questions.explanation: 女生说 'We each potted our own herb garden... They let us take the pots home'，故 C 正确。A 是 wrong_detail：种的是香草盆栽，不是设计花园。B 是 related_but_not_stated：堆肥从未被提及。D 是 related_but_not_stated：种树在对话中没有出现。

questions.stem: What does the woman suggest the man do?

options.A: Register early for next month's class
options.B: Buy his herbs from a local nursery instead
options.C: Ask the instructor for private lessons
options.D: Join her in the greenhouse this weekend

questions.explanation: 女生说名额很快满，建议 'grab a spot early if you want in next month'，故 A 正确。B 是 unrelated_next_step：她从未建议去苗圃买香草。C 是 over_inference：没有提到私人课程。D 是 wrong_detail：课程在下个月，不是本周末和她一起去。

items.situation: a student asking a friend for recommendations on a new couch for the dorm

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You got a couch for your dorm last semester, right? I want one, but mine has to fit through that narrow doorway.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, that's exactly why I didn't go with a big sectional. I got a futon that folds flat, so it slid right in.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, a futon. Is it comfortable enough to actually sit on every day, though?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Mine is, but honestly the cheap ones sag fast. I'd spend a bit more and check the frame is metal, not wood.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay. And where did you even find one that wasn't huge?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The home store on Fifth Street has compact ones. I'd measure your doorway first, then go look this weekend.

questions.stem: Why is the woman talking to the man?

options.A: To complain that her current couch is uncomfortable
options.B: To ask him to help move furniture into her dorm
options.C: To find out where the nearest furniture store is
options.D: To get advice on choosing a couch for her dorm

questions.explanation: 女生说想买沙发，并不断向有经验的男生请教款式、质量和购买地点，故主旨是征求买沙发的建议，D 正确。A 是 reversed_situation：她还没有沙发，谈不上抱怨现有的不舒服。B 是 wrong_topic：从未请他帮忙搬家具。C 是 too_narrow：商店位置只是众多建议中的一条，不是整体目的。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do before buying a couch?

options.A: Order a large sectional sofa online
options.B: Measure the width of her doorway
options.C: Borrow the man's futon for a week
options.D: Ask the dorm office for a couch

questions.explanation: 男生最后建议 'measure your doorway first, then go look'，而女生整段都担心门窄，可推断她会先量门，故 B 正确。A 是 rejected_option：男生正因门窄而放弃大型组合沙发，她也不会去订。C 是 unrelated_next_step：没有任何借用对方沙发的提议。D 是 unrelated_next_step：对话中从未提到向宿舍办公室要沙发。

items.situation: a student asking a shop about repairing a cracked phone screen

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I dropped my phone and the whole screen is cracked. Can you fix it here today?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me take a look. Ah, it's a deep crack. We can replace the screen, but we're out of this model's part right now.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh no. How long would it take to get the part in?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: We'd order it overnight, so it'd be ready by tomorrow afternoon. The repair itself takes about an hour once it's here.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That works. Do I need to leave the phone with you now?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: No, hang on to it. I'll text you the moment the part arrives, and you can bring it in then.

questions.stem: Why does the woman go to the shop?

options.A: To have a cracked phone screen repaired
options.B: To buy a brand-new phone of the same model
options.C: To return a phone she recently purchased
options.D: To ask about extending her phone's warranty

questions.explanation: 女生开头就说手机摔了屏幕全裂，问能否修，故 A 正确。B 是 wrong_topic：她要修屏，不是买新机。C 是 reversed_situation：她不是来退货的。D 是 wrong_topic：保修延长从未被提及。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do after this conversation?

options.A: Leave her phone at the shop today
options.B: Buy the replacement part herself online
options.C: Wait for a text before returning to the shop
options.D: Go to a different shop for a faster repair

questions.explanation: 男生说零件到货时会发短信通知她再带手机过来，可推断她会等短信再回店，故 C 正确。A 是 rejected_option：男生明确说 'hang on to it'，不必现在留下手机。B 是 unrelated_next_step：自己网购零件从未被提及。D 是 over_inference：她已说 'That works'，没有迹象要换别家。

items.situation: a student asking the front desk about renewing a gym locker rental

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, my gym locker rental expires this Friday. Can I renew it for next semester?

conversation.speaker: Man
