conversation.text: Sure. Renewals opened on Monday. You can do it right here at the desk or through the recreation app.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, the app would be easier. Is the price still twenty dollars?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It went up to twenty-five this year, but that now includes a towel service.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's not bad, actually. Do I get to keep the same locker number?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Only if you renew before Friday. After that, any unclaimed lockers get reassigned.

questions.stem: How can the woman renew her locker rental?

options.A: Through the recreation app or at the desk
options.B: By emailing the recreation office directly
options.C: By visiting the gym manager in person
options.D: Through a form mailed to her dorm

questions.explanation: 男生说可以“right here at the desk or through the recreation app”办理续租，所以A正确。B、C、D都是对话中没有提到的办理方式，属于话题相关但未陈述的干扰项。

questions.stem: What will probably happen if the woman waits until after Friday?

options.A: She will receive a full refund
options.B: She will pay a lower renewal price
options.C: She may be assigned a different locker
options.D: She will lose access to the gym entirely

questions.explanation: 男生说Friday之后“unclaimed lockers get reassigned”，所以等到周五之后她可能被分到不同的储物柜，C正确。A退款没提到；B价格是涨了不是降；D失去整个健身房使用权属于过度推断。

items.situation: two students comparing notes after a guest lecture on ocean currents

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Did you catch everything in that guest lecture on ocean currents? My notes are a mess.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Mostly. The part about how wind drives the surface currents made sense, but the deep-water section lost me.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Same here. Apparently temperature and salt levels pull the colder water downward.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Right, and that's what keeps the whole system circulating. The professor called it a conveyor belt.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That image actually helps. Want to combine our notes before the quiz on Thursday?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Definitely. Let's meet at the library tomorrow afternoon.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: A disagreement about the lecture's accuracy
options.B: A lecture they are trying to understand together
options.C: A research project on ocean temperatures
options.D: A change to their Thursday quiz schedule

questions.explanation: 两人都说没完全听懂讲座，正在互相帮助理解内容，所以B正确。A他们并没有争论讲座对错；C不是在做研究项目；D只是顺带提到周四的quiz，并非主旨。

questions.stem: What will the speakers most likely do next?

options.A: Email the professor for the lecture slides
options.B: Listen to the whole lecture again next week
options.C: Skip the quiz scheduled for Thursday
options.D: Meet at the library to combine their notes

questions.explanation: 女生提议合并笔记，男生说明天下午在图书馆见面，所以D正确。A找教授要slides没提到；B重听整场讲座属于过度推断；C跳过quiz与对话相反。

items.situation: a student asking a librarian how to open an online journal from off campus

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Excuse me, I'm trying to open a journal for my history paper, but the site keeps asking me to pay.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Are you connecting from off campus? The library subscription only works through our own system.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'm at my apartment, yeah. So I can't read it from home at all?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You can, but you have to log in through the library's proxy link first, using your student ID.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, I didn't even know that existed. Where do I find the link?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: It's on the database page, top right corner. Bookmark it for next time.

questions.stem: Why can't the man open the journal from home?

options.A: His student account has already expired
options.B: The journal is no longer subscribed to
options.C: He is not connecting through the library system
options.D: The database is down for maintenance

questions.explanation: 女生指出图书馆订阅只在“our own system”里有效，他从校外直接连所以要付费，C正确。A账号过期、B不再订阅、D系统维护都未在对话中提到。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do next?

options.A: Use the proxy link to log in with his ID
options.B: Pay the fee to read the article online
options.C: Choose a different topic for his paper
options.D: Go to the library to read it in person

questions.explanation: 女生告诉他用proxy link加学生ID登录，并让他收藏链接，所以他最可能用proxy link登录，A正确。B付费与建议相反（rejected）；C换题目无依据；D去图书馆现场读未提及。

items.situation: Two friends discuss whether to join a campus club or an intramural team.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'm trying to figure out how to meet more people this semester. I'm thinking about joining either the photography club or the intramural volleyball team.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Both sound fun. What's holding you back?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Well, the volleyball team practices three evenings a week, and that overlaps with my chemistry lab. The photography club only meets on Sundays.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Then it kind of decides itself, doesn't it? You can't skip lab.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right. But I really wanted something more active. Sitting all day is getting to me.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: You could still do the club and just start running with me in the mornings. We jog by the river before classes.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's a good call. I'll come along tomorrow and see how it feels.

questions.stem: What are the two students mainly discussing?

options.A: Which campus activity the woman should join
options.B: How to improve the woman's chemistry grade
options.C: Where the photography club takes its photos
options.D: Why the volleyball team changed its schedule

questions.explanation: 对话围绕女生在摄影社和排球队之间如何选择展开，A正确。B错：化学实验只是排球练习时间冲突的原因，不是讨论主题（too_narrow）。C错：对话从未提及摄影社去哪里拍照（wrong_topic）。D错：没有谈到排球队改时间，是误读情境（reversed_situation）。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: Quit her chemistry lab to play volleyball
options.B: Stop trying to meet new people this semester
options.C: Join the photography club and jog in the mornings
options.D: Sign up for the intramural volleyball team

questions.explanation: 女生接受了男生的建议——参加周日的摄影社，同时早上跟他一起跑步，C正确。A错：她明确说不能翘实验课（rejected_option）。D错：排球队和实验课冲突，她已放弃（rejected_option）。B错：她的初衷就是认识更多人，不会放弃，属于过度推断（over_inference）。

items.situation: Two students compare their reactions to a novel they both read.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So you finally finished the novel for Professor Adler's seminar. What did you think of the ending?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, it frustrated me. The author leaves it totally open whether the narrator is telling the truth.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: See, that's exactly what I loved. It made me want to reread the whole thing looking for clues.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm. I guess I prefer a story that resolves things. But maybe I missed the point.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I wouldn't say that. We're just reading for different reasons. You want closure; I want a puzzle.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Fair enough. Actually, now I'm curious what those clues were. Maybe I'll skim it again before class.

questions.stem: What is the conversation mainly about?

options.A: Whether the narrator of the novel lied
options.B: The students' differing opinions of the novel's ending
options.C: How long it took the man to finish reading
options.D: Why Professor Adler assigned the novel

questions.explanation: 整段对话是两人对小说开放式结局的不同看法的交流，B正确。A错：叙述者是否说谎只是结局的一个细节，不是整体主题（too_narrow）。C错：阅读用时从未被讨论（wrong_topic）。D错：没有谈教授为何布置这本书，是另一个话题（wrong_topic）。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the man by the end of the conversation?

options.A: He now dislikes the novel even more
options.B: He plans to drop Professor Adler's seminar
options.C: He thinks the woman misunderstood the book
options.D: He is becoming more open to the woman's view

questions.explanation: 男生最后说被勾起了好奇心、想重读找线索，说明他开始接受女生欣赏开放式结局的视角，D正确。A错：他从沮丧转为好奇，并未更讨厌（reversed_situation）。B错：没有任何退课的迹象（over_inference）。C错：是男生自己说‘也许我没读懂’，并未指责女生误解（misattributed/over_inference）。

items.situation: Two friends recommend a podcast to each other.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you found anything good to listen to on your commute lately?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Actually, yeah. There's this history podcast called Buried Lines. Each episode digs into one forgotten invention.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Ooh, that sounds right up my alley. How long are the episodes?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: About twenty minutes each, so it's perfect for a short bus ride. The host explains everything really clearly.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Twenty minutes is ideal. My bus trip is exactly that long.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Then start with the one on early refrigeration. It's the episode that got me hooked.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Refrigeration it is. I'll queue it up for tomorrow morning.

questions.stem: According to the man, what is each episode of the podcast about?

options.A: A famous historical figure
options.B: A current news event
options.C: One forgotten invention
options.D: A different country's history

questions.explanation: 男生明确说每集深入讲一个被遗忘的发明，C正确。A错：节目讲的是发明，不是人物，改了关键事实（wrong_detail）。B错：是历史节目，不是时事新闻（wrong_detail）。D错：节目按发明分集，不是按国家，未提及（related_but_not_stated）。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do tomorrow morning?

options.A: Listen to the refrigeration episode on the bus
options.B: Record her own history podcast
options.C: Recommend the podcast to the man
options.D: Walk to campus instead of taking the bus

questions.explanation: 女生说要为明早排上那集（早期制冷），且她的公交车程正好二十分钟，A正确。B错：她只是收听，没有要自己做节目（over_inference）。C错：是男生向她推荐，方向反了（misattributed）。D错：她计划坐公交时听，没有改成步行（unrelated_next_step）。

items.situation: A student returns a too-small sweater at a store counter.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'd like to return this sweater. I ordered it online, but it's a bit too small.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: No problem. Do you have the receipt or the order confirmation?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I've got the email confirmation right here on my phone.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect. Would you like a refund, or would you rather exchange it for a larger size?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'd love the next size up, actually. Do you have it in store?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me check the back. If we don't have it here, I can have it shipped to your home for free.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That'd be great, thanks.

questions.stem: Why does the woman want to return the sweater?

options.A: It is the wrong color
options.B: It arrived damaged
options.C: She found a cheaper one
options.D: It is too small for her

questions.explanation: 女生一开始就说毛衣有点太小，D正确。A错：她没说颜色不对（wrong_detail）。B错：没有提到毛衣有损坏（related_but_not_stated）。C错：她从未提及找到更便宜的（related_but_not_stated）。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do next?

options.A: Give the woman a full cash refund
options.B: Check the stockroom for a larger size
options.C: Cancel the woman's original order
options.D: Direct her to a different store

questions.explanation: 男生说‘让我去后面查查’，即去库房看有没有大一号，B正确。A错：女生选择换货而非退款（rejected_option）。C错：没有提到取消原订单（unrelated_next_step）。D错：他说店里没有就免费寄到家，不是让她去别的店（over_inference）。

items.situation: two friends debating whether a new cafe is worth the price

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you tried that new cafe on Elm Street yet? The one with the fancy lattes?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I went once. The coffee's good, but honestly, six dollars for a latte feels steep on a student budget.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Six dollars? Wow, that's almost double what the campus cart charges.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. The atmosphere's nice, sure, but I can't justify paying that every single morning.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, maybe it's more of a once-in-a-while treat than a daily spot.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Right. I'll stick with the cart on weekdays and save Elm Street for special occasions.

questions.stem: How much does a latte cost at the new cafe?

options.A: About six dollars each
options.B: About three dollars each
options.C: Around ten dollars each
options.D: The same as the campus cart

questions.explanation: 男士明确说新咖啡馆的拿铁要六美元，所以A正确。B项三美元大约是校园咖啡车的价格，属于改动关键数字的错误细节；C项十美元是凭空编造的错误数字；D项说和校园咖啡车价格一样，但对话里女士说新咖啡馆几乎是咖啡车的两倍，属于信息误用。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do?

options.A: Stop buying coffee to save money entirely
options.B: Buy a latte from the new cafe every morning
options.C: Visit the new cafe only for special occasions
options.D: Ask the cafe to lower its prices

questions.explanation: 男士说平时喝校园咖啡车的咖啡，只在特殊场合去Elm Street，可推断他只会偶尔光顾新咖啡馆，故C正确。A项完全戒掉咖啡属于过度推断；B项每天早上买拿铁正是他明确拒绝的做法；D项要求咖啡馆降价与对话内容无关。

items.situation: two friends recommending a new detective TV series to each other

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You have to watch that new detective series everyone's talking about, Harbor Lines. I finished it in two nights.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Two nights? That good, huh? I've been looking for something to watch over the break.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: It's brilliant. Each episode is only thirty minutes, so it never drags, and the ending completely surprised me.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I usually avoid long shows because I lose interest halfway through.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Then this is perfect for you. Short episodes, tight plot. Just don't read anything online first, you'll spoil the twist.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Got it, no spoilers. I'll start it tonight after I finish this assignment.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: How to finish assignments before the break
options.B: A TV series the woman recommends to the man
options.C: Why the man dislikes long television shows
options.D: A show the man convinces the woman to watch

questions.explanation: 整段对话是女士向男士推荐一部她刚看完的侦探剧，故B正确。A项完成作业只在结尾一带而过，不是主题，属于错误话题；C项男士不喜欢长剧只是他接受推荐的一个原因，范围过窄；D项方向相反，是女士向男士推荐，而非男士说服女士。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do?

options.A: Recommend the show to his entire class
options.B: Read reviews online before he watches it
options.C: Cancel his break plans to watch television
options.D: Begin the series without looking up spoilers

questions.explanation: 男士说今晚做完作业就开始看，并回应了女士别看剧透的建议，可推断他会在不查剧透的情况下开始看剧，故D正确。A项向全班推荐属于过度推断；B项看剧透正是女士警告、男士答应避免的做法，属于被拒绝的选项；C项取消假期计划与对话无关。

items.situation: two students chatting about a pottery class one of them took

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: How was that pottery class you signed up for at the community center?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, it was messier than I expected, but I loved it. We learned to use the pottery wheel.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: The wheel? That sounds hard. Did anything actually survive?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: My first bowl collapsed completely. But the second one came out great, and the instructor's firing it in the kiln this week.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's exciting. So when do you get to pick it up?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Friday. I'm already thinking about signing up for the next level.

questions.stem: What did the man learn to use in the class?

options.A: A painting easel
options.B: A sewing machine
options.C: A pottery wheel
options.D: A kiln for glass

questions.explanation: 男士明确说这门课学的是使用陶艺转盘（pottery wheel），故C正确。A项画架和B项缝纫机都与陶艺课无关，属于错误细节或未提及的内容；D项虽然对话里提到窑（kiln），但那是老师用来烧制成品的，并不是男士学着操作的东西，属于信息误用。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do?

options.A: Sign up for a more advanced pottery class
options.B: Open his own pottery business someday
options.C: Buy a pottery wheel for his room
options.D: Switch to a painting class instead

questions.explanation: 男士说他已经在考虑报名下一个级别的课程，可推断他会继续报更进阶的陶艺课，故A正确。B项自己开陶艺公司属于过度推断；C项给房间买转盘、D项改上绘画课都与他表达的意图无关，属于无关的后续动作。

items.situation: two friends coordinating a weekend beach trip

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So, are we still on for the beach on Saturday? I've been looking forward to it all week.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Definitely. But the parking lot near the pier fills up fast, so we should leave early.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: How early are we talking?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: If we're out the door by seven, we'll beat the crowd and get a good spot on the sand.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Seven it is. Should I bring the cooler and drinks?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll grab the umbrella and towels, and I'll drive us there.

questions.stem: What time do the friends plan to leave?

options.A: At six in the morning
options.B: Around eight o'clock
options.C: As soon as the lot opens
options.D: By seven in the morning

questions.explanation: 男士说如果七点前出门，就能避开人群并占到好位置，所以计划七点前出发，故D正确。A项六点和B项八点都是被改动的错误时间；C项停车场一开门就走在对话中没有提到，属于貌似合理但未陈述的内容。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely bring to the beach?

options.A: The umbrella and the towels
options.B: A cooler and some drinks
options.C: Nothing, since the man is driving
options.D: Beach chairs and sunscreen

questions.explanation: 女士主动提出带冷藏箱和饮料，男士也答应了，可推断她会带这些，故B正确。A项遮阳伞和毛巾是男士负责带的，属于信息误用；C项因为男士开车就什么都不带属于过度推断；D项沙滩椅和防晒霜对话中从未提及。

items.situation: two friends planning a surprise birthday party for a classmate

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, so Dan's birthday is next Thursday. I really want to throw him a surprise party.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Love it. The tricky part is keeping it secret, he always figures these things out.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I know. That's why I'm thinking we tell him we're just studying at the library, then bring him to Maria's place instead.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Clever. So he thinks it's an ordinary study night. But how do we get everyone there before him?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: We ask them to arrive by six, and I'll stall Dan with coffee until six-thirty.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Nice. I'll message the group chat tonight, but I'll leave Dan out of it, obviously.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly planning?

options.A: A surprise birthday party for their friend
options.B: A study session at the library before an exam
options.C: How to invite people to a coffee meetup
options.D: A birthday party they want Dan to organize

questions.explanation: 两人整段都在策划如何给Dan办一场惊喜生日会，故A正确。B项去图书馆学习只是他们用来骗Dan的幌子，并非真正的主题，属于错误话题；C项约人喝咖啡只是拖住Dan的一个细节，范围过窄；D项方向相反，派对是为Dan办的，不是让Dan来组织。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do tonight?

options.A: Book a venue and order a cake tonight
options.B: Invite Dan to the group chat directly
options.C: Text the others but keep Dan uninformed
options.D: Ask Maria to cancel the whole party

questions.explanation: 男士说今晚会在群里通知大家，但会把Dan排除在外，可推断他会联系其他人却不告诉Dan，故C正确。A项订场地和蛋糕对话中没提到，属于过度推断；B项直接把Dan拉进群正是他明确说要避免的做法，属于被拒绝的选项；D项让Maria取消派对与计划完全相反、毫无依据。

items.situation: two students swapping music recommendations because one is bored of the same pop singers

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'm so bored of the same pop singers on every playlist lately. Can you recommend something completely different?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Definitely. Have you tried that indie folk band, River and Stone? Their new album is really mellow and great for studying.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that sounds nice. I've never heard of them. Are they on the usual streaming apps?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, they're everywhere. Start with the track called 'Harbor Lights' — that one hooked me right away.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll queue it up during my study session tonight.

questions.stem: What does the man recommend the woman listen to?

options.A: A new album by an indie folk band
options.B: A pop singer's latest single
options.C: A playlist of classical study music
options.D: A live concert happening this weekend

questions.explanation: 男生明确推荐独立民谣乐队 River and Stone 的新专辑。B项与女生厌倦流行歌手的心情相反，是错误细节；C项对话未提及古典乐单；D项现场演唱会对话中完全没有提到。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do tonight?

options.A: Delete all of her old pop playlists
options.B: Buy tickets to see the band live
options.C: Listen to the recommended track while studying
options.D: Ask the man for even more suggestions

questions.explanation: 女生说她今晚学习时会把这首歌加入播放队列，采纳了男生的推荐。A项属过度推断，她只是厌倦流行乐并未说要删歌单；B、D两项对话中都没有依据。

items.situation: two students talking about which elective class to take next term

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you decided which elective you're taking next term? I'm torn between photography and film studies.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I already signed up for the photography one. Professor Nguyen teaches it, and everyone says her feedback is amazing.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's good to know. Does it meet in the morning or afternoon? My mornings are packed.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's Tuesday and Thursday afternoons, so you'd be fine. There were only a couple of spots left when I checked, though.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Then I'd better register today before they fill up.

questions.stem: What does the man say about the photography elective?

options.A: It is already fully booked
options.B: Professor Nguyen gives great feedback
options.C: It meets on Monday mornings
options.D: It requires an expensive camera

questions.explanation: 男生说大家都说 Nguyen 教授的反馈很棒。A项错误，他说只剩几个名额而非满员；C项错误，课在周二周四下午；D项关于昂贵相机对话未提及。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Switch to a morning film studies class
options.B: Ask the professor for permission to join
options.C: Wait until next term to decide
options.D: Register for the class right away

questions.explanation: 女生说趁名额没满今天就去注册，说明她会立刻报名。A项与她想避开满课的清晨相反且属她放弃的选项；B项对话没有依据；C项与她急着报名的态度相反。

items.situation: two students figuring out what dish to bring to a department potluck

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So for the department potluck, what should the two of us actually bring? I don't want us both showing up with dessert.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Right. The sign-up sheet already has tons of desserts and chips. What's really missing is anything with substance.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: True. Maybe a big pasta salad? It travels well and we could make it the night before.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That works, but half the class is vegetarian, so let's skip the versions with meat in them.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Good thinking. I'll draft a simple recipe and send it over so we can split the shopping.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: Whether to attend the potluck at all
options.B: How to split the cost of groceries
options.C: What dish to bring to the potluck
options.D: Which classmates have dietary restrictions

questions.explanation: 两人整段都在商量给系里聚餐带哪道菜。A项与他们已经计划参加相反；B项分摊费用只是结尾一句次要提及；D项素食者只是决定菜品时的一个考量，均非对话主旨。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the pasta salad they plan to make?

options.A: It will not contain any meat
options.B: It will be bought ready-made from a store
options.C: It will be the only savory dish there
options.D: It will be prepared at the party itself

questions.explanation: 因为一半同学吃素，两人决定跳过含肉的版本，可推断这道意面沙拉不放肉。B项与他们打算自己前一晚做相反；C项属过度推断；D项错误，他们说是前一晚在家做好。

items.situation: two friends deciding whether to keep their shared room or apply for singles next year

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you thought about housing for next year? I can't decide if we should keep this room or apply for singles.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I've been going back and forth too. The double is cheaper, but honestly the noise from the lounge next door drives me crazy.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Same here. A single would be quieter, but the application deadline is this Friday, which is really soon.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Wait, this Friday? I thought we had until the end of the month.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Nope, they moved it up this year. We should talk it through tonight so we don't miss it.

questions.stem: According to the woman, what is true about the singles application?

options.A: It costs less than a double room
options.B: It has already closed for the year
options.C: It needs a roommate's signature
options.D: Its deadline is this coming Friday

questions.explanation: 女生说单人间的申请截止日提前到了本周五。A项张冠李戴，是双人间更便宜；B项错误，截止日尚未到；C项关于室友签名对话未提及。

questions.stem: What will the speakers most likely do tonight?

options.A: Submit the singles application immediately
options.B: Discuss their housing decision together
options.C: Complain to the residence office about noise
options.D: Move their belongings to a new room

questions.explanation: 女生提议今晚把这件事谈清楚，两人尚未做出决定，所以最可能的是一起商量。A项属过度推断，他们还没决定；C、D两项对话中都没有依据。

items.situation: a customer asking a tailor shop to hem dress pants before an interview

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I bought these dress pants for an interview, but they're a few inches too long. Can you hem them?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Of course. For a simple hem like this, it's twelve dollars, and we can have them ready by Thursday.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Thursday? Oh no, my interview is Wednesday morning. Is there any way to get them sooner?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: We do offer a rush service for an extra eight dollars. If you leave them now, they'll be done tomorrow afternoon.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That would be a lifesaver. Let's do the rush option, then.

questions.stem: What does the woman want the shop to do?

options.A: Shorten the length of her pants
options.B: Replace a broken zipper on her pants
options.C: Remove a stain from her dress pants
options.D: Take in the waist of her pants

questions.explanation: 女生说裤子长了几英寸，想让店里改短，也就是缝边。B项换拉链、C项去污渍对话都未提及；D项收腰是错误细节，她说的是长度不是腰围。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: Pick up the pants on Thursday as first offered
options.B: Look for another tailor nearby
options.C: Pay extra for the rush service
options.D: Wear different pants to her interview

questions.explanation: 女生说"Let's do the rush option"，即愿意多付钱用加急服务。A项是她因面试在周三而放弃的选项；B、D两项对话中都没有依据。

items.situation: two students arranging to study together before a final exam

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, are you free to review for the statistics final together this weekend? I'm completely lost on the probability chapter.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'd love to, but I'm working both days at the campus store. Could we do a video call on Sunday night instead?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Actually, a call might be tricky with all the formulas. What if I record my questions and send them, then we meet Monday morning before the exam?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm, Monday's cutting it close, but okay. Let's grab the group room at eight so we've got two hours to work through everything.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll book it right now.

questions.stem: Why is the woman talking to the man?

options.A: To plan when they can study for the final together
options.B: To ask him to explain the probability chapter right now
options.C: To reschedule his weekend work shifts
options.D: To cancel studying together entirely

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：女生开口就说想约周末一起复习统计学期末，整段都在敲定时间，这是对话主旨。B错在“太窄”：概率章节只是她说自己没学懂的一个细节，不是她找对方的目的，而且她要的是约时间而非现在讲解。C错在“话题错误”：男生要上班是他提出的约束，女生并没有要帮他调班。D错在“情境相反”：她是在积极安排一起复习，而不是取消。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Call the man on Sunday night
options.B: Skip the probability chapter entirely
options.C: Reserve the group study room
options.D: Email the professor for extra time

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：女生最后说“I'll book it right now”，指的就是预订两人约定八点使用的小组自习室。A错在“已被否决”：女生明确说打电话对着一堆公式不方便，两人改成周一见面。B错在“过度推断”：她正是因为概率没懂才要复习，绝不会直接跳过。D错在“无关的下一步”：对话里从未提到向教授申请额外时间。

items.situation: two friends debating whether a new cafe is worth the price

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you tried that new cafe on Elm Street? Everyone keeps posting about it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I went yesterday. Honestly, the latte was amazing, but it cost almost six dollars. That's double what we pay at the campus coffee cart.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Six dollars? Wow. Is the atmosphere at least nice enough to study there?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's the thing — it's gorgeous, tons of natural light and comfy chairs, but it gets really loud in the afternoons.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm. So great coffee, bad for studying. I think I'll just go once as a treat.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, that's probably the smart move.

questions.stem: What does the man say about the cafe's coffee?

options.A: It tastes worse than the campus cart's coffee
options.B: It is excellent but expensive
options.C: It is cheaper than he expected
options.D: It is only served in large sizes

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：男生说拿铁“amazing”但要将近六美元，是校园咖啡车的两倍——味道好但贵，正好对应。A错在“改了关键事实”：他夸咖啡好喝，没有说更难喝。C错在“改了关键事实”：他强调价格贵、是两倍，不是比预期便宜。D错在“对话未提及”：从没说过只有大杯。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the woman's attitude toward the cafe?

options.A: She will visit the cafe daily for coffee
options.B: She plans to study there despite the noise
options.C: She intends to ask for a student discount
options.D: She views it as an occasional treat rather than a regular stop

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：女生听到又贵又吵后说“I'll just go once as a treat”，说明她把它当偶尔的享受，而非常去的地方。A错在“过度推断”：她明说只去一次，不可能天天去。B错在“过度推断”：男生说下午很吵不适合学习，她也认同“bad for studying”，不会去那学习。C错在“无关的下一步”：对话里根本没提学生折扣。

items.situation: two students talking about a new downtown restaurant and which dish to try

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So I finally checked out that new Thai place downtown. The reviews weren't kidding.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh nice! What did you order? I've been wanting to go, but I can never decide.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I got the green curry, which was good, but honestly my roommate's mango sticky rice stole the show. You have to try the dessert.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Really? I usually skip dessert. Was it too sweet?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Not at all — perfectly balanced. Skip the appetizers, though; they were overpriced and pretty small.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good to know. I'll definitely save room for that dessert.

questions.stem: What does the woman recommend the man try?

options.A: The green curry
options.B: The appetizers
options.C: The mango sticky rice dessert
options.D: A Thai iced tea

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：女生说室友的芒果糯米饭“stole the show”，还直接说“You have to try the dessert”，明确推荐这道甜点。A错在“改了关键事实”：绿咖喱她只说“good”，并没推荐，抢镜的是甜点。B错在“改了关键事实”：她让男生跳过开胃菜，因为又贵又小。D错在“对话未提及”：泰式冰茶从没被提到。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do when he goes to the restaurant?

options.A: Make sure to order the dessert
options.B: Order several of the appetizers
options.C: Ask the woman to come along with him
options.D: Complain to the staff about the prices

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：男生虽然平时不吃甜点，但听完推荐后说“I'll definitely save room for that dessert”，可推断他会特意点甜点。B错在“已被否决”：女生提醒跳过开胃菜，他不太可能反而多点。C错在“无关的下一步”：对话没提约女生一起去。D错在“过度推断”：他只是了解到价格，从未表示要投诉。

items.situation: two roommates working out a fair chore and quiet-hours schedule

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, can we figure out a cleaning schedule? The kitchen's been a mess all week.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, sorry about that. How about I take the dishes and trash, and you handle the bathroom and vacuuming?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That works. Let's just swap every two weeks so it stays fair.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good call. Oh, and can we set quiet hours? I've got an early class and you're up pretty late.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Sure. How about no loud music after eleven?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll write it all on the whiteboard.

questions.stem: According to their agreement, what will the man be responsible for?

options.A: Vacuuming the apartment
options.B: Cleaning the bathroom
options.C: Doing the grocery shopping
options.D: Washing the dishes and taking out the trash

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：男生主动提出“I take the dishes and trash”，即负责洗碗和倒垃圾。A错在“改了关键事实”：吸尘是女生的任务。B错在“张冠李戴”：打扫卫生间也分给了女生。C错在“对话未提及”：两人从没谈到买菜采购。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do next?

options.A: Go buy new cleaning supplies
options.B: Write the schedule on the whiteboard
options.C: Move his early class to a later time
options.D: Look for a different roommate

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：男生最后说“I'll write it all on the whiteboard”，会把清洁分工和安静时段写到白板上。A错在“无关的下一步”：对话没提去买清洁用品。C错在“过度推断”：他只是想设安静时段，没说要改课，本来也改不了排课。D错在“过度推断”：两人是在友好协商分工，完全没有要换室友的意思。

items.situation: two friends figuring out a bus schedule for a day trip to the coast

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: If we want to reach the coast by noon, which bus should we catch on Saturday?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The express leaves at nine and gets there in about two hours. The local one takes almost four.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Nine's fine. Wait, does the express even run on weekends? Some routes only go on weekdays.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good point. Let me check the app... yeah, it runs Saturdays, but not Sundays.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Great, so Saturday it is. Should we buy tickets ahead of time?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Definitely. I'll book them online tonight before they sell out.

questions.stem: How long does the express bus take to reach the coast?

options.A: About two hours
options.B: Almost four hours
options.C: Just under an hour
options.D: It depends on the traffic

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：男生说快车九点发车，约两小时到，正好对应。B错在“张冠李戴”：将近四小时是慢车（the local one）的时间，不是快车。C错在“改了关键事实”：没有“不到一小时”这种说法。D错在“对话未提及”：两人没谈到路况会影响时长。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do tonight?

options.A: Take the local bus instead of the express
options.B: Cancel the trip to the coast
options.C: Purchase the tickets online
options.D: Check the weather forecast for Saturday

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：男生说“I'll book them online tonight before they sell out”，今晚就会在网上买票。A错在“已被否决”：两人选的是九点的快车，慢车更慢，没被采用。B错在“过度推断”：他们已确定周六出行，没有取消之意。D错在“无关的下一步”：对话里从没提到查天气。

items.situation: two students talking about which elective class to take next term

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you figured out which elective you're taking next term? I'm stuck between Intro to Film and Environmental Science.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I already signed up for Environmental Science. My roommate took it last year and said the weekend field trips are the best part.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Field trips? That actually sounds fun. Does it meet more than once a week, though? I've got a packed schedule.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Just Tuesdays and Thursdays, plus one Saturday trip a month. It's really manageable.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, the Saturday thing might clash with my part-time job. Let me check my shifts before I decide.

questions.stem: According to the man, what is the best part of the Environmental Science class?

options.A: The monthly weekend field trips
options.B: The small class size
options.C: The Tuesday and Thursday lectures
options.D: The friendly professor

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：男生转述室友的话，说“the weekend field trips are the best part”，即周末的野外考察是最棒的部分。B选项错误，对话从未提到班级人数。C选项错误，周二和周四是上课时间，但男生并没有说这是最棒的部分。D选项错误，对话没有提到教授是否友好。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Enroll in Environmental Science immediately
options.B: Quit her part-time job to free up Saturdays
options.C: Check her work shifts before deciding
options.D: Sign up for Intro to Film instead

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：女生最后说“Let me check my shifts before I decide”，明确表示要先查看自己的兼职排班再做决定。A选项错误，她还没有决定报名，属于过度推断。B选项错误，她只是担心周六可能冲突，从未打算辞掉兼职。D选项错误，对话没有任何信息支持她会转而选电影课。

items.situation: a student asking a shop about repairing a cracked phone screen

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, my phone screen cracked when I dropped it this morning. Can you tell me if it's fixable?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me take a look. Okay, the glass is cracked but the display underneath still works, so a screen replacement should do it.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, good. How much will that run me, and how long does it take?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's forty dollars, and we can usually finish within two hours. But we're out of the part for your model right now.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Out of stock? When will you have it?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: A new shipment arrives tomorrow morning. If you drop it off then, it'll be ready by lunchtime.

questions.stem: Why does the woman go to the shop?

options.A: To buy a brand-new phone
options.B: To find out if her cracked screen can be repaired
options.C: To complain that her display stopped working
options.D: To ask for a refund on an earlier repair

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：女生开门见山说手机屏幕摔裂了，问“Can you tell me if it's fixable?”，主旨是想知道裂屏能否修复。A选项错误，她要修的是现有手机，不是买新机。C选项错误，男生检查后说屏幕下的显示仍然正常，属于对情况的反向误读。D选项错误，对话没有涉及退款。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: Have her phone repaired later today
options.B: Buy the part from another store
options.C: Ask the staff for a discount on the repair
options.D: Bring her phone back the next morning

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：男生说配件现在缺货，明早到货，只要那时把手机送来，中午就能修好，所以女生最可能第二天早上再把手机拿来。A选项错误，配件缺货，今天无法修，属于被明确排除的选项。B选项错误，对话没有提到去别的店买配件。C选项错误，没有任何信息表明她会要求打折，属于过度推断。

items.situation: two students swapping music recommendations because one is bored of the same pop singers

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, I'm so bored of my playlist. It's the same three pop singers on repeat and I need something new.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: You should check out this indie folk band I've been into lately. They've got these really mellow harmonies, totally different from mainstream pop.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Indie folk? I've never really listened to that. Is it easy to get into?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Definitely. I'll send you a link to their first album tonight. Start there and see what you think.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That'd be great. I'm always up for discovering something outside my usual bubble.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: How to build a better music playlist app
options.B: A concert the two of them attended together
options.C: Finding new music beyond the woman's usual pop
options.D: The man's strong dislike of folk music

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：女生说厌倦了老听同样的流行歌手，想要新东西，男生随即推荐一支独立民谣乐队，整段对话围绕帮她发现流行乐之外的新音乐。A选项错误，对话没有谈论制作播放器应用。B选项错误，两人没有一起看过演唱会。D选项错误，男生恰恰喜欢并推荐民谣，属于对情况的反向误读。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: Listen to the album the man recommends
options.B: Delete her entire current playlist
options.C: Start her own indie folk band
options.D: Buy tickets to the band's concert

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：男生说今晚会把乐队第一张专辑的链接发给她，女生欣然答应并说愿意尝试新东西，所以她最可能去听男生推荐的专辑。B选项错误，她只是厌倦老歌单，从未说要删掉整个歌单，属于过度推断。C选项错误，组建自己的乐队远超对话内容。D选项错误，对话没有提到演唱会或买票。

items.situation: two friends discussing whether to join a campus club or intramural team

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you decided whether you're joining the photography club or the intramural soccer team? You can't do both, right?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, they both meet on Wednesday evenings, so I have to pick one. I'm leaning toward soccer for the exercise.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That makes sense. The photography club does sound cool, though. Don't they have that darkroom workshop?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: They do, but honestly I've been sitting at a desk all semester. I really need to get moving more.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Fair enough. Sounds like your mind's basically made up.

questions.stem: Why can't the man join both groups?

options.A: They both cost too much money
options.B: He isn't skilled at either activity
options.C: Both groups are already full
options.D: They both meet on Wednesday evenings

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：男生明确说“they both meet on Wednesday evenings, so I have to pick one”，因为时间冲突所以只能二选一。A选项错误，对话没有提到费用。B选项错误，男生没有说自己不擅长，只是想多运动，属于改动关键事实。C选项错误，对话没有提到名额已满。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do?

options.A: Join the photography club
options.B: Sign up for the intramural soccer team
options.C: Attend the darkroom workshop
options.D: Quit both groups to focus on studying

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：男生说自己整学期都坐在桌前，需要多运动，倾向于选足球，所以最可能报名校内足球队。A选项错误，他因为想运动而实际上放弃了摄影社，属于被排除的选项。C选项错误，暗房工作坊属于摄影社，也在他放弃之列。D选项错误，退出两者去学习没有任何依据，属于过度推断。

items.situation: two friends discussing a movie one just saw — good twists but too long

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I finally watched that new mystery everyone's raving about, 'Silent Harbor.' The twist at the end completely floored me.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, I've heard the ending's amazing. Worth checking out this weekend?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: For the writing, absolutely. But fair warning, it's nearly three hours, and the middle really drags.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Three hours? I don't think I could sit still that long without a break.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, you might want to watch it at home so you can pause partway through.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: You know, that's not a bad idea. I'll just stream it once it's available online.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: A movie the woman found excellent but overly long
options.B: The woman's plan to write a mystery novel
options.C: Whether a theater ticket is worth the price
options.D: A movie that both speakers strongly disliked

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：女生盛赞这部悬疑片的剧本和结尾反转，但也提醒它将近三小时、中段拖沓，整段对话就是围绕这部“很好看但太长”的电影。B选项错误，女生只是看了悬疑片，并没有要写小说。C选项错误，对话没有讨论电影票价是否值得。D选项错误，女生其实很喜欢这部电影，属于对情况的反向误读。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do?

options.A: Watch the movie in the theater this weekend
options.B: Read some reviews before deciding
options.C: Wait and stream the movie at home
options.D: Ask the woman to explain the twist

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：男生说三小时坐不住，采纳女生的建议，说“I'll just stream it once it's available online”，所以他会等上线后在家观看。A选项错误，他因为时长而明确放弃了去影院这周看，属于被排除的选项。B选项错误，对话没有提到看影评。D选项错误，让女生解释反转没有任何依据，也不符合他的下一步计划。

items.situation: two students talking about a new restaurant downtown and which dish to try

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you heard about Saffron Kitchen, that new Thai place downtown? I tried it last night.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, I've been meaning to go! Everyone keeps raving about it online. Is it as good as they say?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, better. But skip the pad thai — it's nothing special. The green curry with crab is what you want.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Green curry, got it. I'm not great with spicy food, though. Should I be worried?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You can ask them to make it mild. Just tell your server when you order — they're really flexible.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll head over Friday after my exam, then. Thanks for the tip!

questions.stem: What is the conversation mainly about?

options.A: The woman is recommending a dish at a new restaurant.
options.B: The man is complaining that the restaurant is too expensive.
options.C: The woman is warning the man to avoid the restaurant.
options.D: The man is describing a meal he cooked at home.

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：对话核心是女生向男生推荐这家新泰餐厅，尤其推荐蟹肉青咖喱这道菜。B选项错误，男生没有抱怨价格，全程没提到贵。C选项错误，女生是在推荐而非警告他别去，她说这家店“比传闻还好”。D选项错误，对话谈的是餐厅点菜，男生并没有描述自己在家做的饭。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do when he goes to the restaurant?

options.A: Order the pad thai as his main dish.
options.B: Avoid the restaurant because of the spice.
options.C: Ask the server to make his dish less spicy.
options.D: Invite the woman to come along on Friday.

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：男生说自己不太能吃辣，女生建议他点菜时让服务员做成微辣，男生接受了建议，所以他很可能会请服务员减辣。A选项错误，女生明确说别点pad thai，男生也决定点青咖喱。B选项错误，男生并没有因怕辣而放弃去，反而计划周五去。D选项错误，对话没有提到他会邀女生同去，女生已经去过了。

items.situation: two students comparing notes on a novel they both read for a literature class

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Did you finish 'The Salt Path' for lit class? I couldn't put it down last weekend.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I did! I read the whole thing in two sittings. The ending really surprised me, though.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right? I did not expect the sister to be the narrator all along. So clever.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. Although I found the middle section a little slow — all those long descriptions of the coast.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Fair. So are you going to write your essay on the ending twist, then?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I think so. It's the most interesting part to analyze. What about you?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'll probably focus on the setting. See you in seminar!

questions.stem: What did the man dislike about the novel?

options.A: The surprising ending.
options.B: The slow middle section.
options.C: The identity of the narrator.
options.D: Its very short length.

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：男生说他觉得中间部分有点慢，全是对海岸的冗长描写。A选项错误，男生喜欢那个出人意料的结尾，被它惊到。C选项错误，叙述者身份是女生盛赞“巧妙”的点，男生也没表示不满。D选项错误，男生没说小说太短，反而说是分两次读完的整本书。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely write his essay about?

options.A: The novel's coastal setting.
options.B: The slow pacing of the book.
options.C: The author's earlier novels.
options.D: The twist at the end of the story.

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：男生说他打算写结尾的反转，因为那是最值得分析的部分。A选项错误，写海岸/背景的是女生。B选项错误，节奏慢是男生的抱怨点，但他没说要以此为题。C选项错误，对话完全没提到作者的其他作品。

items.situation: two students figuring out a time to meet for a history group project

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: We really need to nail down a time to meet for the history project. Are you free Thursday afternoon?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Thursday's tough — I've got my lab until four, and then I coach intramural soccer at five.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay. What about Wednesday, right after lunch? Say, one o'clock in the library?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Wednesday at one works for me. Should we book a group study room so we're not whispering?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Good idea. I'll reserve room 204 online tonight. Can you bring the source notes?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure, I'll print them out beforehand. See you Wednesday.

questions.stem: When do the students agree to meet?

options.A: Thursday afternoon.
options.B: Thursday at five o'clock.
options.C: Wednesday at one o'clock.
options.D: Wednesday evening.

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：女生提议周三午饭后一点在图书馆见，男生回答“Wednesday at one works for me”，两人达成一致。A选项错误，男生周四下午有实验课到四点。B选项错误，周四五点男生要带足球队。D选项错误，约的是周三一点，不是周三晚上。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do tonight?

options.A: Reserve a group study room.
options.B: Print out the source notes.
options.C: Cancel her Thursday lab class.
options.D: Email the history professor.

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：女生明确说“I'll reserve room 204 online tonight”，所以她今晚会在线预订小组自习室。B选项错误，打印资料是男生的任务。C选项错误，有实验课到四点的是男生，且没人要取消课。D选项错误，对话没有提到给教授发邮件。

items.situation: two friends deciding whether to buy concert tickets now or wait

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So, should we grab tickets for the indie festival now, or wait? They just went on sale this morning.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I checked earlier — the lawn seats are already ninety dollars. That's steeper than last year.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Ninety? That's more than I expected. I heard prices sometimes drop closer to the date if it doesn't sell out.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Maybe, but this lineup is huge. I'd bet it sells out by the weekend, honestly.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yeah, you're probably right. I don't want to miss it to save twenty bucks.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Same. Let's just buy them tonight before they're gone. I'll send you the link.

questions.stem: How much do the lawn seats currently cost?

options.A: Twenty dollars.
options.B: The same as last year.
options.C: Nineteen dollars.
options.D: Ninety dollars.

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：男生说他查过了，草坪区座位已经要九十美元。A选项错误，二十美元是女生说的“不想为省二十块而错过”里的数字，不是票价。B选项错误，男生说今年比去年更贵，并非一样。C选项错误，是九十不是十九，属数字混淆。

questions.stem: What will the two friends most likely do?

options.A: Wait for the ticket prices to drop.
options.B: Buy the tickets tonight.
options.C: Choose seats other than the lawn.
options.D: Attend a different festival instead.

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：两人担心演出会在周末前售罄，男生提议“今晚就买，趁还没卖完”，女生也同意，所以他们很可能今晚下单。A选项错误，他们讨论后否决了等降价的想法。C选项错误，对话没有提到换其他区域的座位。D选项错误，没人提议改去别的音乐节。

items.situation: a student returning a too-small sweater at a store counter

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I'd like to return this sweater. I bought it last week, but it turned out to be a size too small.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: No problem at all. Do you have your receipt with you?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I do, right here. Can I get a refund back to my card?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Of course. Since it's within thirty days and unworn, I can refund the full amount to your original card.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Great. Actually, do you have this same sweater in a medium? I still really like it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me check the stockroom for you. One moment.

questions.stem: Why does the woman want to return the sweater?

options.A: It is a size too small for her.
options.B: It has a hole in the sleeve.
options.C: She no longer likes the color.
options.D: It was the wrong item entirely.

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：女生开头就说这件毛衣小了一码，所以要退。B选项错误，对话没提到毛衣破了。C选项错误，女生反而说她还很喜欢这款，想换中码。D选项错误，她要退的正是自己买的那件，只是尺码不合，不是拿错货。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do next?

options.A: Refuse the return without a receipt.
options.B: Charge her a restocking fee.
options.C: Look for a medium in the stockroom.
options.D: Send her to a different store branch.

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：女生问是否有中码，男生说“Let me check the stockroom for you”，所以他接下来会去库房找中码。A选项错误，女生已经出示了收据，不存在无收据拒退。B选项错误，男生说三十天内未穿可全额退款，没有提到收取任何手续费。D选项错误，对话没有让她去别的分店。
